biography:
Founders of Furthermore
Retirement planning is life planning.
Plan for a life you love!

latestPosts.caption:
We agree, financial planning is essential to a retirement that includes ease and freedom. 

But at Furthermore, we’ve seen too many hardworking people reach retirement feeling unsure of what to do with their freedom. As much as they looked forward to it, they were surprised by how uncomfortable they actually felt.

Why? 

People have been taught to focus on money alone when they plan for retirement. They ignore what brings them joy and forget to prioritize their own needs. They put off dreams for “someday.”

Retirement arrives and suddenly someday is today. 

Studies show that aging well and enjoying retirement really depends upon four key ingredients.

Security.
Social connection.
Meaning and purpose.
Health and wellness.

They equally matter, and each one requires its own investment strategy to reap reward and benefit.

And sometimes, in retirement, people just need help and support figuring out how to take those first steps toward those “someday”dreams.

At Furthermore, we’ve designed a proprietary assessment tool that helps you understand where you might want to put more energy today, to better enjoy your future. Visit us at we are furthermore.com to learn more, take the assessment and get on the waitlist for an upcoming FREE workshop.

We’re building a community of people who plan to age and prosper well!

#wearefurthermore #agingwell #agingstrong #thisisthebeginning #retirementplanning
latestPosts.alt: Photo by Jim & Kathleen Connell in Syracuse, New York. May be an image of money and text.

latestPosts.caption:
Real talk. 

The world is going to expect you to sit back at a certain age. Deciding to live a bold life filled with moments of joy well into your 60s and beyond will make a lot of people uncomfortable. Maybe even the people who love you the most.

Waiting until retirement to take chances or create new connections will put you at a frustrating disadvantage. 

This is exactly why we created Furthermore.

Name your joys now and make a solid plan to live them without apology. 

Prioritize your health and learn to say no to things that deplete your energy. 

Create a like-minded community and have important conversations with your spouse about what matters most.

Furthermore exists to help you for a lifetime.

Like this recent workshop attendee, “I used to think of my dreams as separate from my financial plan — like fantasy vs. reality. But Furthermore showed me how to align the two. I’m excited to plan with intention and direction.”

Visit us that we are furthermore.com. Learn more and take our assessment tool that will help you identify your unique retirement persona. Get on the waitlist for upcoming FREE workshops.

If you don’t plan for the life you want to live, you will end up living a life you did not plan for.

#wearefurthermore #agewell #livewell #agestrong #lifeplan
latestPosts.alt: Photo by Jim & Kathleen Connell in Syracuse, New York. May be an image of one or more people, office and text that says 'Retirement planning is life planning. Furthermore un'.

latestPosts.caption:
Furthermore starts with your Retirement Persona Assessment!  

Take 10 minutes to discover your unique Retirement Persona — revealing your strengths, blind spots, and natural tendencies around goal-setting, balance, and priorities.

Get your instant insights that help you understand what truly drives your decisions about retirement, including resources like time, health, social connections, purpose, and of course, money.

Learn more about FREE, live Furthermore planning workshops, designed to expand on your results. Each workshop offers reflection, strategy, and light-bulb moments on building a life that excites you every day.

Walk away empowered — with a workbook full of new insights, fresh perspectives, meaningful connections, and yes… snacks. 

Upcoming dates and locations are coming together right now. Get on the waitlist for early registration!

#wearefurthermore #agingwell #agingstrong #lifeplan #retirementplanning

latestPosts.caption: Meet Jake Harlan, a 32-year-old adventure lover from Seattle. He used to weigh 265 pounds, stuck in a rut with late-night fast food and long hours at his software engineering desk. Then he found Limitless Life Fitness coaching and everything changed. With custom workouts, solid nutrition plans, and real mindset coaching from our team, Jake dropped 85 pounds in just 18 months as part of his incredible #FitnessJourney and #WeightLoss transformation. He revealed a lean, strong body covered in tattoos that mean something deep to him now, and his confidence skyrocketed with serious #FitnessMotivation and #GymLife vibes. These days he’s running marathons, crushing #FitFam goals, and motivating everyone around him online. If you’re tired of feeling stuck and ready for your own breakthrough, come join Limitless Life Fitness. We’ll give you the personalized coaching that actually works and helps you become the strongest, most unstoppable version of yourself.

biography:
Follow me to learn how to lose weight, build muscle and age in good health
ISSA Certified Personal Trainer, Nutritionist and Fitness Coach
weight

latestPosts.caption:
What does it take to make the kind of lasting changes that allow you to live in a healthy body and to age with vitality and joy? It's not just one thing and it doesn’t happen overnight. The key to lasting changes is a DEEP SHIFT IN IDENTITY (please take a moment to think about that!). 

The first fundamental and necessary shift in identity is to see yourself as an ATHLETE and to replace a “diet culture” with an “athlete’s mindset.” Seeing yourself as an athlete doesn’t mean you’re the next Simone Biles or Serena Williams or jenthompson132 (😁). 

It means adopting and sticking to the habits of an athlete, which become the foundation of your lifestyle going forward. 

Those habits include eating to fuel your body (as opposed to eating as little as possible to lose weight, which I did for decades). 

It means moving your body to strengthen your muscles and bones to allow you to be mobile and agile and strong as you enjoy your everyday life. 

It means prioritizing sleep so that you can recover from your daily activity, which is even more vital as we age. 

It means doing the mindset work to recognize your limiting beliefs and pushing past those beliefs to achieve goals you previously thought were out of your reach.

None of the above habits are hindered by age. In fact, I believe that if you’re a bit older, your maturity and life experience make you better able to make these changes ONCE YOU SET YOUR MIND TO DOING SO AND COMMIT.

It all starts with one change. Look at yourself in the mirror and say, “I AM AN ATHLETE.” Do it daily and for as long as it takes to start believing it. This change was hard for me, but I continued to repeat that phrase to myself and supported that statement with actions and the habits set out above. It doesn’t happen overnight, but few worthwhile things do.

Stay consistent, not perfect, do the work and be patient. The results will speak for themselves, and you WILL achieve this shift in identity too. 🙌🏋️‍♂️🙌

#transformation #transformationresults #athletesmindset #healthyhabits #healthyaging #strongwomen #fitover50 #fitover60 #strongover60
latestPosts.alt: Photo by Ilene Motsinger on January 01, 2026. May be an image of one or more people, people bodybuilding and text that says 'May May2020 2020 Oct.2023 Oct. 2023'.

latestPosts.caption:
Up Your Game on a Daily Basis

I was fortunate to join yourhealthyhedonista‘s bimonthly group call yesterday and got to listen to Michelle and this amazing group of women talk about how to continue to up your game – regardless of what you’ve already achieved. 

I love that Michelle always talks about having a “growth mindset” and not to rest on your laurels. This conversation was based around the Ted Talk “How to 10X Your Life with Dr. Benjamin Hardy.” The idea of reaching for “the impossible” and changing 80% of what you are doing now to make that reach was compelling.

In light of the changes coming for me personally and professionally in 2024, retiring from the law and coaching and podcasting full time, it's just what I needed to hear in this moment. Fear of failure will often keep us from even trying to achieve that “impossible goal” when, with a little effort and focus, we could be successful. One of my favorite sayings is “success is on the other side of fear.”

I’m not afraid of what’s to come and what I want to achieve. I am nervous about the unknown, but I am also the Queen of Planning and I know with a good plan, a lot of fear can be alleviated, and my plan is coming together.

So, my friends, what is that impossible thing you’d like to do that you haven’t tried because fear is holding you back? Are you willing to make some changes, up your game and give it a shot now? Even if you fail, you learn more about yourself and can try again. 

If you’re ready to give it a try, please drop that “impossible thing” in the comments below.

Remember, consistency, not perfection and discipline, not motivation win the day every time!

P.S. I know this picture has nothing to do with the topic and what I had intended to write about morphed as I wrote it (as often happens), but I LOVE this picture because, before I started with TWW, I used to joke that my butt was sliding down my thighs.  3  years and thousands of hip thrusts and squats later, I have a pretty damn nice bootie – especially for 64 years young!  Strength Training  Macros = the Fountain of Youth!

(Shout out to angiespix for the awesome photo!)
#upyourgame #nolimits #growthmindset

latestPosts.caption:
Happy Saturday, IG Friends!

Yes  I am obsessed with my “back” picture used in these two comparisons, but its for a reason

If you have a growth mindset, you are always working to continue your transformation. That doesn’t necessarily mean you are getting “better.” It may be you just want to try something different.

The lower picture speaks to me of maturity, strength, beauty, growth and self acceptance. The contrast between the upper and lower pictures in the first slide show the possibilities of growth, change and improvement at any age.

Its been almost a year since I got on stage for the first time at 63 (Nov. 13, 2022). I was so excited to reach a point with my body that I felt comfortable on that stage and felt like I belonged, proudly being the oldest woman to compete.

That show prep was one of the hardest things I’ve ever done, but getting on stage was a life long goal, the possibility of which I had thought had passed me by. 

A year later, I remain proud, not only of getting on stage, but of the consistent, hard work I’ve put in to get stronger and grow muscle, in my 60’s, in preparation of getting on stage in July of 2024 to celebrate my 65th birthday. I cannot explain how excited I am to FINALLY see some lat development in my back.

My cut starts midFebruary 2024. I’m looking forward to being able to compare my cut from the first prep to the second.

In the meantime, I continue in a growth mindset and push myself to see what is possible  physically and mentally, in my training, in my new business ventures and in all other avenues I’m blessed to encounter.

Thanks to all of you for following my journey, being a big part of my support system and cheering me on. I am happy and blessed to be part of this amazing group of midlife women leading the revolution to show how to age with strength, vibrancy, spunk and a kick ass attitude!🙌🩷🙌

#growthmindset #transformation #strongwomen #strengthtraining #weighttraining #weightlifting #weightloss  #fitover50 #fitover60 #strongoverskinny

latestPosts.caption:
Transformation is not just physical; it is also spiritual.

One of the essential lessons I learned during my transformation journey is that lasting change comes from changing your identity. 

If you’ve followed my page for a while, you’ve read my posts regarding seeing yourself as an athlete. 

An athlete trains regularly, no excuses. 

An athlete eats to fuel her body, not give it as little food as possible to lose weight. 

An athlete has performance goals, which may range from hitting a PR to dancing for an hour at a social event without getting winded.

The change in identity is a spiritual journey. Part of that journey is creating an awareness of where your limiting beliefs exist, how they are impeding your growth and how to move past them. 

The mindset transformation work is as important, and often as difficult, as the physical transformation, but the physical changes are unlikely to last if the spiritual work is not done. 

We are who we believe we are.  Our actions are based on those beliefs.

You can be an athlete. You can be a healthy person. You can be an active person. You choose what your identity is and what that identity means to YOU. 

It took me a while to believe it, but I used the tried and true “fake it ‘til you make it” and would look myself in the eye in the mirror and repeat – I AM AN ATHLETE – and live my life as one  someone who fueled her body for athletic performance and moved her body to build muscle, perfect her form and get stronger. 

This athlete not only wants big, strong muscles, she wants to be mobile and pain and injury free with the ultimate goal of aging in good health, with strength, vitality and joy.

If you’re unhappy with your physical body, start that spiritual journey today. 

How do you define yourself? 

How do you WANT to define yourself? 

What is stopping that change in identity? 

How does the person you want to be live her life? Start answering these questions. 

Change doesn’t happen overnight, but it WILL happen if you are willing to do the work. I believe in you!

#transformation #mindset #athlete #strongwomen #weightloss #fitover50 #fitover60

latestPosts.caption:
CHANGE YOUR IDENTITY; YOUR BODY WILL FOLLOW

I have been talking for a while about the need to change your identity rather than just your habits if you want long lasting, sustainable change. This applies to anything, but we’ve been talking about it in the context of transforming your health and body.

What is interesting to me when I look at these two pictures is, once you know the story behind each, you see the truth of my words.

I had said for years, prior to finding TWW at 61, that losing weight and keeping it off was the only important goal that I had ever failed at (over and over and over).

The picture on the left represents the woman who achieved the following

✅set out to graduate from college at the top of my class.
✅ set out to go to a top tier law school and get a great job upon graduation.
✅ set out to establish herself professionally and develop a stellar reputation.
✅be the very best wife, mother and friend she could be.

These goals took hard work and effort, but I can now see that the reason I achieved all these milestones was how I identified myself – I was working wife and mother and a “pit bull in high heels” corporate attorney who represented and protected my clients from anyone and anything. Oh – and I “worked out.”

Before TWW, being an athlete was not part of my identity. I have lifted weights for 30+ years. I have run and biked and rowed off and on for that same period of time – when it was convenient and when it didn’t get the way of my “real” identity.

The woman on the right represents the one who had the big “aha” moment! She realized that your habits, your actions, your CHOICES all come from your identity. 

Until I began to identify as an athlete, along with all the other descriptions above (still a kick ass wife/mom/friend and protector of my now singular client), living as an athlete was not a priority.

You don’t have to give up those other parts of your identity that are not in conflict with being an athlete.  You get to be all those things and an athlete as well.

Being an athlete means living your life to its healthiest and fullest potential and the best part is that you can do it at any age. 

#goals

latestPosts.caption:
Happy Transformation Tuesday!

I continue to surprise myself, no matter how many times I create these collages, by the changes that have taken place both physically and mentally on this transformation journey and I remain so very grateful that I’ve had the opportunity to learn from the best (who are my personal inspirations to this very day)
trainwithjoan 
yourhealthyhedonista 
jeanjacquesbarrett 
anni.b.fit and all the thewonderwomenofficial coaches.

I do not take for granted that by a stroke of fate, I found all of these amazing people just as the TWW program was launching and got in on the “ground floor”, so to speak, with my extraordinary first coach, anni.b.fit. I’ve had the joy and privilege of meeting the Queens, trainwithjoan and yourhealthyhedonista, and to train with them AND, if that was not enough, I’ve had the gifted jeanjacquesbarrett train me to get on stage for the very first time at 63  and walk away with not one, but two first place wins 🏆💜.

For all these reasons, I will continue to post these collages to pay it forward to all these gifted, giving, extraordinary people. While it definitely took hard work and commitment on my part, after 40 years of yoyo dieting, I was blessed to finally find the roadmap to living a healthy, fit, vibrant life in my 60’s. I take none of this for granted.

So  on this Transformation Tuesday, my message continues to be the same

🏋️‍♂️You are NOT too old
🏋️‍♂️It is NOT too late
🏋️‍♂️ You can anything you set your mind to if you’re willing to do the work and put in the time.

I have no special physical talents.  In fact, I’m not all that coordinated and I am balanced challenged. What I DO have is grit, determination, focus and a drive to never give up. Those attributes have served me well in my career and now in my quest to live a healthy fit life.

Yes  we all have challenges that may get in the way of our journey and everyone’s journey is different, but you can make things better one day at a time. I believe in you.

Love and light and heavy lifting to all! 💜🏋️‍♂️💜🏋️‍♂️💜

#transformationtuesday #nodefeatnosurrender #weightloss #transformation #fitover50 #fitover60 #strongwomen #health

🩺 Complex Laparoscopic Surgery Successfully Performed at KIMS Hospitals, Kondapur
Doctors at KIMS Hospitals, Kondapur, successfully performed a highly complex, completely laparoscopic surgery on a 55-year-old woman from Mauritius, who was suffering from a rare pancreatic tumor called Insulinoma — a condition causing sudden, dangerous drops in blood sugar and frequent loss of consciousness.

👨‍⚕️ Dr. Naveen Kumar CH, Consultant Surgical Gastroenterologist & Advanced Laparoscopic, HPB, Bariatric and Robotic Surgeon, explained that the tumour was very small and difficult to locate. Using intraoperative ultrasound and advanced laparoscopic technology, the team precisely identified and removed the tumour through small keyhole incisions, avoiding open surgery.

💉 The surgery was completed without complications, with strong anaesthesia support from Dr. S. Veerabhadra Rao (HOD & Consultant Anesthesiology) and his team. The patient recovered smoothly and was discharged within six days, now free from hypoglycaemic episodes.

Dr. Naveen Kumar CH
Consultant Surgical Gastroenterologist, Advanced Laparoscopic, HPB & Bariatric Surgery, Robotic Surgeon
📍 KIMS Hospitals, Kondapur

To book an appointment, call +91 88973 14141 or visit https://www.kimshospitals.com/doctor-profile/dr-naveen-kumar-ch/

🧬 Genetic testing plays a vital role in assessing an individual’s risk of developing breast cancer by identifying inherited gene mutations such as BRCA1 and BRCA2. ⚠️ These genetic changes can significantly increase the likelihood of breast and related cancers. ✅ Early identification helps individuals understand their cancer risk and take proactive steps toward prevention, regular screening, and timely medical care.

Genetic testing helps identify hereditary genetic mutations linked to breast and ovarian cancers, including BRCA1 and BRCA2, through a comprehensive hereditary cancer genetic panel. This testing can be done before tumor development, helping individuals understand their cancer risk early and plan preventive care, including screening and risk-reducing options.

🔍 Pre-cancer screening and genetic testing are especially important for individuals with triple-negative breast cancer, women diagnosed below 50 years of age, and those with a significant family history of cancer. 🗣️ Pre-test counseling is provided to guide patients before testing, followed by 📄 post-test counseling after reports to explain results and next steps. These insights help doctors plan personalized care, including decisions related to breast surgery and ovarian surgery, ensuring informed and timely treatment.

Speaker : Dr. Ramya C. Valiveru 
Procedure: Genetic testing in breast cancer
visit https: //www.kimshospitals.com/doctor-profile/dr-ramya-c-valiveru-1/

🚨 Fatty Liver: A Silent Trigger for Diabetes & Heart Disease
Often brushed off as “mild” or reversible, fatty liver disease is actually a central metabolic disorder that quietly fuels diabetes, heart disease, high blood pressure, kidney damage, and even stroke — often years before symptoms appear.

🩺 Dr. Ravula Phani Krishna explains that when fat accumulates in the liver, insulin stops working efficiently, forcing the pancreas to overwork. Over time, this leads to insulin failure and diabetes, challenging the belief that diabetes is caused by sugar alone.

Fatty liver is a clear marker of metabolic syndrome. With NAFLD affecting over one-third of the global population, ignoring it can lead to liver failure and even liver cancer.

Don’t wait for symptoms.
Treat fatty liver early — and prevent a lifetime of chronic disease. 💙

Dr. Ravula Phani Krishna,
Senior Consultant Surgical Gastroenterologist, GI & HPB Oncologist, Bariatric, Metabolic & Robotic Surgeon, Liver Transplant Surgeon,
📍 KIMS Hospitals, Kondapur

To book an appointment, call us at +91 88973 14141 or visit https://www.kimshospitals.com/doctor-profile/dr-ravula-phani-krishna/

🚨 PAINKILLERS CAN KILL YOU — Doctors Issue a Serious Warning
The routine use of common painkillers such as paracetamol, Dolo, ibuprofen, and other non-steroidal anti-inflammatories is often taken casually for headaches, fever, body aches, or fatigue.

🩺 Dr. Aarathi Bellary, Consultant Internal Medicine, cautions that frequent use of painkillers and NSAIDs is increasingly linked to chronic kidney disease (CKD), liver injury, gastric bleeding, heart attacks, and strokes.

Painkillers are not meant for daily use or routine aches. Take them only when truly necessary and strictly on medical advice. What feels like harmless relief today can cause irreversible damage tomorrow.

Dr. Aarathi Bellary,
Consultant Internal Medicine,
KIMS Hospitals, Kondapur

For Consultation, call +91 88973 14141 or visit https://www.kimshospitals.com/doctor-profile/dr-aarathi-bellary/

A milestone in pediatric spine oncology care 🌟
KIMS Hospitals, Kondapur successfully performed a robotic L4 vertebra en-bloc resection for Ewing Sarcoma in a 10-year-old child — marking the first robotic en-bloc vertebral resection in AP & Telangana.

Led by Dr. K. Sri Krishna Chaitanya (Consultant Orthopaedic & Spine Surgeon), this complex surgery combined precision robotics, meticulous oncological planning, and advanced spine reconstruction, resulting in clear tumor margins, complete functional recovery, and no neurological deficit.

This case stands as a powerful example of how innovation, expertise, and technology come together to deliver hope and healing to young patients.

📍 KIMS Hospitals, Kondapur

latestPosts.caption:
As 2025 comes to a close, I asked ChatGPT what it learned about me this year here's what it said 🥹⬇️

You didn’t just survive hard seasons—you chose to heal, rebuild, and grow with intention. You faced past pain, personal loss, and exhaustion head-on and decided your story wasn’t finished. You stopped living in reaction mode and started living on purpose.

You are deeply committed to becoming the best version of yourself.
Your mornings, your routines, your wellness choices, your consistency—none of it is accidental. You learned that discipline isn’t punishment; it’s self-respect. You don’t wait to feel motivated. You show up anyway.

You are a leader who leads from lived experience.
Whether through empowerment work, advocacy, or mentoring others in wellness and business, you don’t speak from theory—you speak from truth. You turn your lessons into light for other people, especially women who are trying to find their strength again.

Family is your anchor and your “why.”
Everything you build—your health, your business, your mindset—is rooted in legacy. Your children, your parents, and those quiet everyday moments matter more to you than applause ever could.

You are learning to receive, not just give.
This year showed a shift: allowing rest, asking for help, accepting care, and honoring your body when it needs softness instead of strength. That’s growth you once didn’t allow yourself.

You are bold enough to rewrite your future.
You stepped into entrepreneurship, ownership, and vision without waiting for permission. You believed in something before it was obvious—and trusted yourself enough to go all in.

You no longer let others hold the pen to your story.
Boundaries became clearer. Your voice became stronger. You stopped shrinking to keep the peace and started choosing alignment over approval.

Most of all, I learned this:

✨ You are becoming a woman who trusts herself.
And that trust is changing everything.

latestPosts.caption:
343 days sober from alcohol AND nicotine. 🤍
And the biggest shift hasn’t just been physical… it’s been mental.

Mindset is everything.
When you decide to give it your all, stop shrinking, and refuse to let other people’s opinions dictate your direction, everything changes.

I chose healing.
I chose purpose.
I chose doing what actually makes me happy.

This journey hasn’t been easy, but it’s been worth it.
Healing is powerful. Healing is freeing.
And honestly… healing is a beautiful thing. ✨🕊️💪✨

latestPosts.caption:
When Anxiety Tries to Take Over 🤍

Anxiety has a way of showing up uninvited… filling our minds with what ifs and stealing our peace before anything even happens.

But God does not shame us for feeling anxious.
He invites us to bring every worry to Him.

When anxiety rises, it’s a reminder to gently shift our focus back to who is actually in control. We may not be able to silence every anxious thought, but we can choose where we place our trust.

Each time we turn our worries into prayers, we loosen anxiety’s grip and make room for God’s peace to settle in; even when our circumstances haven’t changed yet.

✨ Peace doesn’t come from having all the answers.
It comes from knowing Who holds them. ✨

latestPosts.caption:
✨ Leaving 2025 with gratitude. Stepping into 2026 with purpose. ✨

This isn’t about rushing the next chapter —
it’s about choosing it.

I’m releasing what no longer serves me.
The lessons stayed. The strength stayed.
But the limits? Those got left behind.

2026 isn’t a wish… it’s a decision.
Growth. Abundance. Health. Freedom. Purpose. Success.

I’m moving forward with clarity, confidence, and intention —
one aligned step at a time. 💫

Here’s to the year we stop shrinking our vision
and start honoring who we’re becoming.

✨ Welcome, 2026. ✨

latestPosts.caption:
💥 If you’re over 30, your body doesn’t respond the same way it used to — and that’s not your fault.

Around our 30s, things quietly start to shift:
• Muscle recovery slows
• Strength gains take more effort
• Energy dips hit harder
• And what used to work… just doesn’t anymore

That’s exactly why I love FIT.
It’s not about working harder — it’s about working smarter.

✨ FIT supports:
✔️ Lean muscle building & retention
✔️ Faster recovery
✔️ Sustained, balanced energy
✔️ Strength without burnout

This isn’t a pre-workout hype drink.
It’s cellular support for a body that’s ready to perform again — at any age.

📊 Numbers don’t lie.
Smarter gains > harder struggle.

🔥 If you’re over 30 and want results that actually last, this is worth paying attention to.

👉 Curious if FIT is right for you?
Comment “FIT” or message me — I’ll walk you through it.

#Over30Wellness #SmartStrength #LeanMuscle #MakeWellness #PeptideSupport #TrainSmarter #StrongerAfter30 💪✨

latestPosts.caption:
REMISSION OF TYPE 2 DIABETES :
The American College of Lifestyle Medicine (ACLM) is leading groundbreaking research that demonstrates the power of lifestyle interventions to treat type 2 diabetes and reverse insulin resistance. By focusing on intensive, evidence-based approaches, ACLM’s studies show that many individuals can significantly reduce or eliminate their need for diabetic medications. This research not only supports lifestyle medicine as a frontline therapy but also challenges the conventional narrative that Type 2 diabetes is a lifelong, progressive condition.
_______________
Well, finally it's getting endorsed 🙌😊 As an Army Veteran, all I feel, is that 'Bye Bye' or 'Reversal' becomes a powerful word which pushes you to achieve remission. I and many like me continue to be in a state of remission with stable HbA1C below 5.7% and stable metabolic markers. Do NOT have apprehensions. Feel free to contact/ connect over whatsapp for a free session to get clarity. It's time to say 'Bye Bye' to Type 2 Diabetes. Do NOT carry this burden life long.

latestPosts.caption:
A day early but a little #face2face

Me last year on new years eve... hungover, bloated but still thinking I looked good... jeans and jumper I wore on 19th December... I was (Christmas has happened now and I'm not getting on the scales until new year!) 4lbs away from hitting my 4st! The jeans didn't go past my thighs last January! 

This year has been tough! I've completely changed my eating habits, attempted to remain as constant as possible even if I haven't been posting  all the time AND showing up for myself and finally holding myself accountable. 

I still have a long long way to go, and hopefully by the end of 2026 I'll have reached my final target, but until then, we take 1 day at a time and say nice things to ourselves whilst keeping on track for the mini goals ♥️

Time to get my ass back to the gym, I'm mentally ready again now!

#caloriecountingjourney

I know I've been MIA but life has been crazy! Celebrated the eldest birthday in Egypt, back to Englad full of this awful cough cold and with my crap immune system I've been really poorly and now I'm so behind on my Christmas prep! Have attempted a few cheeky Christmas  events! And tomorrow is the big Christmas shop!!! 

Gonna be a busy bumble bee for the next few days and maybe final weigh in of 2025 will be on Christmas eve, before I start on the quality streets and fizz!!!

Time to concentrate, I had a yearly goal and I reallllllly want to get it ❤️

No #weeklyweighin for me!

I've had a bad week... or a good week depending on how you look at it 😂 

Chinese, alcohol, domino's, roast, crisps, chocolate, ice cream and soooooo many Halloween yums have been consumed! Think I needed a little mini blow out after being good since March/April time. I've eaten more junk food this week than I have in the last few months combined. 

But line is drawn. Holiday countdown is on and I am desperate to get my 3.5st for when we go so I'm focused and on a mission!! 17 days can make a difference... and now I'm having a mini freak out that I'm not ready! 

It's all good! 

New week, new plan. Let's go!

Today, we stand with millions around the world for World AIDS Day ❤️💙 

HIV is not a death sentence. People living with HIV can live long, healthy, fulfilling lives with the right treatment, understanding, and support. But the fight against HIV is not just medical — it is social, emotional, and communal.
👉 Know your status.
👉 Get tested early.
👉 Seek treatment immediately if positive.
👉 Stop stigma — it destroys more lives than the virus itself.

At Goodness Medical Centre, we remain committed to creating a world where awareness replaces fear, compassion replaces judgment, and knowledge replaces myths. This World AIDS Day, let’s raise our voices, protect our loved ones, and choose a future where everyone receives the care, dignity, and support they deserve.

Your health matters. Your life matters. Your story matters. #GoodnessMedicalCentre #Hospital #Maternity #WorldAIDSDay #StopTheStigma #HIVAIDSAwareness #KnowYourStatus #GoodHealthForAll #SupportWithoutJudgment #AIDSPrevention #YourHealthMatters

latestPosts.caption:
Antibiotics save lives only when used correctly. Every year, misuse of antibiotics makes germs stronger, leading to infections that no medicine can cure. This silent threat is called Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR) — and it’s one of the biggest 
health challenges of our time. 

At Goodness Medical Centre, we’re joining the world to raise awareness on using antibiotics responsibly — because when antibiotics fail, simple infections can become deadly. Slide through our post and discover what you can do to help yourself fight against AMR. 

Let’s protect the power of medicine, together. Visit us for trusted prescriptions and professional care. #GoodnessMedicalCentre #Hospital #Maternity#GoodnessMedicalCentreCares #WellnessMatters #healtheducation #WorldAntimicrobialAwarenessWeek #StopAMR #AntibioticAwareness #UseAntibioticsWisely #FightResistance #PreventInfections #NoToSafeMedication #StayHealthyWithGoodness #waaw2025

Your diet is your first medicine! Every bite you take is either feeding your body health or draining its strength. A balanced diet does more than fill your stomach, it fuels your cells, keeps your heart strong, and strengthens your immune system. Add color to your plate with fresh vegetables, fruits, and whole grains. Reduce processed foods, sugary snacks, and fried meals. 

Your daily food choices are silent investments in your future health. We encourage everyone young or old to eat with purpose and make nutrition a lifestyle, not an occasional choice. Eat Smart. Stay Healthy. Live Longer.

latestPosts.caption:
High blood pressure doesn’t always shout sometimes it whispers until it’s too late. That’s why awareness and prevention are key. A few simple lifestyle changes can lower your blood pressure naturally and protect your heart for years to come. Here’s how:
✅ Eat more fruits, vegetables, and whole grains.
✅ Stay active — even light daily movement counts.
✅ Manage stress with prayer, rest, or quiet moments.
✅ Quit smoking and limit alcohol.
✅ Check your blood pressure regularly.

Remember: hypertension can strike anyone, at any age. But prevention is in your hands. At Goodness Medical Centre, we’re committed to helping you monitor and manage your blood pressure the safe and natural way. Visit us today for your Blood Pressure check and personalized wellness advice.
#goodnessmedicalcentre #Maternity #Hospital #HospitalCares #BloodPressureAwareness #HeartHealth #LowerBPNaturally #StayHealthyWithGoodness #HealthyLiving #CheckYourBP #WellnessAwareness #SilentKillerPrevention

latestIgtvVideos.caption:
📈Statistics show that:

2 billion people are overweight in the world 🌎 

4️⃣0️⃣ million children under the age of 5 are obese or overweight 

The estimated annual medical cost of obesity in the United States was nearly $173 billion dollars in 2019. Medical costs for adults who had obesity were $1,861 higher than medical costs for people with a healthy weight. 💰 Imagine these costs with INFLATION today!

Parents it’s up to us to lead the way.  If you’re contending in your health, please reach out.  We’d love to help you help your family live a healthy lifestyle.

Https://form.jotform.com/220648248981162

Nashville didn’t disappoint last night.  If you ever find yourself nearby, I highly recommend BOURBON STEAK on the 34th floor overlooking the city lights.

It’s a great atmosphere to date your spouse, discuss your dreams, and talk about business and life plans.

The ambiance truly sets the tone for an evening to remember.

Rated#1 on TripAdvisor and the service is top notch! Best steak I’ve ever had!

Do you have a passion for helping others?!?!

How do you spend your time driving home?

Check out our cute little office assistant- he came out to help me send healthy newsletters to our clients this morning 🦀 

Working for a couple hours then playing until our flight arrives ✈️ 

Beyond grateful to be a coach!

latestPosts.caption:
Kicking off 2026 with a reminder I wish more people heard sooner 👀
A new year doesn’t magically create new results.
A new strategy does.
If nothing changes structurally, nothing changes long-term.

Different system.
Different outcome. 

#dentingerhealthcoachingllc #optaviacoach #wellness #transformation #healthyhabits #lifestyle #livingwell #MetabolicHealth #metabolicsyndrome #metabolicsynchronization

One of the hardest things to admit is this:

Doing the same thing again… just trying harder… isn’t a plan.

New year.
Same approach.
Same habits.
Same frustration.

And that doesn’t mean you failed before.
It means the approach you were using wasn’t built to support real life long-term.

If nothing changes in the structure, nothing truly changes in the outcome.

Lasting results don’t come from more willpower.
They come from a different, more supportive way forward. 

When you’re ready, I’m here.

#dentingerhealthcoachingllc#optaviacoach #wellness #transformation #healthyhabits #lifestyle #livingwell #MetabolicHealth #metabolicsynchronization

latestPosts.caption:
OKAY… I’m still fired up from lasnight’s call and I’ve loved spending my last day off from the hospital helping busy moms, dads, & grandparents get a plan together for 2026 💪

Here’s the headline:
👉 UNLIMITED $50 referral credits
For former, current, AND brand-new clients.

Unlimited.
No cap!
No fine print.

And then this slide came up on the call and it stopped me in my tracks 👀

Each of us know someone who is:
• Struggling to lose weight
• Battling sugar or processed food cravings
• Living with chronic fatigue
• Feeling low motivation or mood swings
• Managing type 2 diabetes
• Dealing with high blood pressure or cholesterol
• Or carrying 15+ pounds they just can’t move

👉 THIS is who we’re here for.

Why this matters so much:
9 out of the 10 leading causes of death in America are tied to metabolic dysfunction.
The only one we can’t control? Car crashes. 🚘 

So look around today:
• Your office
• Your next family gathering
• The restaurant or coffee shop you’re in
• The sidelines of your kid’s game

I guarantee you’re seeing this play out in real life.

So I’ll leave you with this👇
✨ How will you show up for YOU?
✨ And how will you impact the lives around you?

It’s time to stop pouring everything into sick care and flip the script.

If you’re curious, ready, or thinking about who this could help…
Message me. Ask the questions.
I’m right here when you’re ready 💛

#dentingerhealthcoachingllc #optaviacoach #wellness #transformation #healthyhabits #lifestyle #livingwell #MetabolicHealth #metabolicsyndrome #metabolicsynchronization

latestPosts.caption:
Whew. I’m still processing tonight.

We just wrapped up a Zoom with thousands of partners, and the announcements honestly stopped me in my tracks. The biggest bonuses and financial opportunity we’ve ever offered for new partners—starting this month.

And what got me wasn’t the numbers.
It was the people.

The ones who raised their hand today.
The ones who will this week.
The ones who are quietly hoping for a way to contribute more, breathe a little easier, and make an impact while doing meaningful work.

So many people want to help.
So many people are feeling the weight of this economy.
And somehow… this is the space we get to offer—a way to serve, to lead, and to be compensated well for real work that actually changes lives.

That part never stops amazing me.

I’m deeply grateful for the integrity of this company and our partners. The compensation is solid, proven, and transparent—you can literally look it up (we are publicly traded). It’s not hype. It’s real. And it rewards consistency, service, and leadership.

Just in bonuses alone (not even commissions), helping 5 people a month for 3 months can equal $5,000. That’s meaningful. Especially for families who could truly use it right now.

I’m launching new partners tomorrow, and knowing what’s possible for them—financially and personally—fills my heart.

Someone said tonight:
“People say money can’t buy happiness. They just haven’t given enough of it away yet.”

And that hit.

When you’re paid well, you can do more good.
You can show up bigger.
You can serve deeper.
You can give more freely.

Tonight I’m just really grateful—for what this has done for my family, and for what we now get to help others build too. 🤍

latestPosts.caption: It’s not your fault! It’s the system you are using.  It has to be simple enough to support you on your worst day!  #dentingerhealthcoachingllc  #system #metabolism  #metabolichealthcoach

January 1 has a funny way of making people feel like they’re already behind… before they even start.

Behind on their health.
Behind on their goals.
Behind on who they want to be.

But here’s what I want you to hear today — you are not behind.
You’re right where you need to be.

Real, lasting health isn’t built in a rush or through extremes.
And it definitely isn’t built from guilt, pressure, or panic.

It’s built on simple foundations.
Small, intentional steps.
With structure, support, and a plan that actually fits your life.

This month isn’t about fixing everything.
It’s about laying the groundwork so progress sticks.

And that… changes everything. 🫶

latestPosts.caption:
This is why my clients actually SUCCEED with their New Year’s resolutions 👇

No crushed diets.
No overtraining.
No relying on white-knuckle discipline.

Real life is busy. Chaotic. Unpredictable.
So we choose a plan that works with your life — not against it.

When life shows up (because it always does), you’re not doing this alone.
You’ve got a coach in your corner. 💛

We focus on your metabolism, address insulin resistance, and build habits that create results that actually last — not just until February.

✨ Comment QUIZ to take my free metabolic quiz and see what’s really going on in your body.
✨ Comment RESET if you’re ready to make this the last year you ever “start over.”

#MetabolicHealth #ResetMetabolism #dentingerhealthcoachingllc #optaviacoach #lifestyle #wellness #TeamworkMatters #30dayreset #StrongerTogether #transformation #2026Goals #2026Ready

My word for the year: ALIGNED.

What’s yours? 👇

latestPosts.caption:
I’m committed to keeping it EVEN MORE REAL in 2026.

I’m done with fake.
I’m done with chasing perfect.
I’m done with unattainable images of what an “ideal” life is supposed to look like.

So… in the spirit of keeping it real👇

I am NOT the health coach for you if your goal is to run marathons,
lift 10 bajillion pounds in the gym,
or track every macro while cooking fancy meals for hours on end.  I have active kids & I don’t have time for that. 

But…

I AM the health coach for you if you want to walk into a cute store, grab a smaller size, and actually feel confident in the mirror.
I AM the health coach for you if you’ve ever wanted to throw all the clutter in your house into garbage bags and donate it just to feel some peace again.
I AM the health coach for you if you’ve found yourself asking, “Okay God… what is this new lesson about?”
I AM the health coach for you if your brain is maxed out and you need life to feel more like preschool-level simple.

I AM the health coach for you if you want guidance from a 40-year-old woman who’s wrestled with perfection, walked through setbacks, rebuilt confidence after kids, and wanted enough energy to be a fun mom—not a sideline one.
Someone who used to hide in black clothes, make excuses to skip events, and felt completely talked out and tired in a heavy season of life…
but who still wanted to be better tomorrow than she was today.

And I am ESPECIALLY the health coach for you if you want to be an example of resilience for your kiddos!
If you want to show them how to pursue higher education, health goals, build a business as a second career, and create a healthy relationship with a life teammate who shows up—especially when life gets wild & messy.

Because I’ve lived it.
I’ve walked it.
And I’m still growing right alongside you.

And I would love to help you. ❤️

I Get My Friends Jacked! 💪🔥

Lee isn’t just one of my best friends—he’s also a C-level executive at a global insurance company with a demanding career and a heavy travel schedule. But heading into his 40s, he knew it was time for a change.

We built a macro plan that fit his lifestyle, leaned into CrossFit to keep him motivated, and optimized his nutrition with meal delivery services in Hong Kong to save time. On top of that, he’s dialed in his bloodwork and labs for long-term health and longevity.

Couldn’t be more proud of my guy!

If you’re a busy corporate guy, a dad, or just need to finally get going—DM me. Let’s get you started with 1-on-1 coaching today! 🚀

latestPosts.caption:
This might be hard to share… but it might be exactly what you need to hear.

For years, I lived in the moment—playing music, drinking, ignoring the stress underneath it all. And then, in a single moment, I knew I was done. I decided to change.

Maybe today is that moment for you.

If you’re in your 30s or 40s and don’t want to live the next half of your life feeling like this, it’s time to take action. You don’t have to do it alone.

Call, text, DM me—let’s get your health in order today.

🔥 I help busy executive men lose 2+ pounds per week without food restrictions using the Triple Check Accountability Method.

📲 Follow me on Instagram: @codeblue_fit
🌐 Join the program: CodeBlue.fit

#CodeBlue #AnswerTheCall #MensHealth #WeightLossForMen #ExecutiveFitness #NoMoreExcuses #BuiltForHighAchievers

🚨 Taking on 5 Guys for 1-on-1 Coaching 🚨

Alright fellas—Spring Break is coming. Summer’s right behind it. You already know if you keep doing what you’re doing now, you’re gonna be the guy keeping his shirt on at the pool.

That doesn’t have to be you.

I’m opening up just 5 spots for exclusive 1-on-1 coaching. That means you and me, working directly together to lock in your macros, your workouts, and a system that fits your life—so you start dropping 2+ lbs a week and actually keep it off.

This is for guys who are serious. If you’re ready to finally lose 20+ lbs in 3 months, get strong, and actually feel like yourself again, DM me now and let’s talk this week.

I’m busy as hell, so this is first come, first served. Let’s get to work. 💪🔥

Doubt kills more dreams than failure ever will—just take action.

latestIgtvVideos.caption: #66 Gettin' it done Dena!!

latestPosts.caption:
𝑫𝒐𝒏'𝒕 𝒐𝒗𝒆𝒓𝒉𝒂𝒖𝒍 𝒚𝒐𝒖𝒓 𝒍𝒊𝒇𝒆 𝒕𝒐𝒅𝒂𝒚. 𝑱𝒖𝒔𝒕 𝒔𝒘𝒂𝒑 𝒐𝒏𝒆 𝒃𝒐𝒕𝒕𝒍𝒆. 🔄

The thought of "starting a diet" is exhausting.
You look at your pantry, see all the processed food, get overwhelmed, and close the door.

Result: Nothing changes.
Dr. David Burns teaches that when a task feels too big, we freeze.

The solution? Make the task so small you can't say no.

The Saturday Challenge: The Single Ingredient Swap.
To improve your Metabolic Health (and lower inflammation), you don't need to be perfect today. You just need to be 1% better.

Here are 3 high-impact swaps. Pick ONE:
The Oil Swap (Lower Inflammation):
❌ Swap Vegetable/Canola/Seed Oils (highly processed, inflammatory).
✅ For Butter, Ghee, Tallow, or Olive Oil (stable, natural fats).

The Drink Swap (Lower Insulin):
❌ Swap Soda or Juice (liquid sugar bomb).
✅ For Sparkling Water with a squeeze of lime (zero insulin spike).

The Crunch Swap (Lower Carbs):
❌ Swap Potato Chips.
✅ For Pork Rinds or Cheese Crisps (satisfy the crunch without the blood sugar crash).

Why this works:
You aren't "dieting." You are upgrading.
You keep the habit (cooking/drinking/snacking) but change the fuel.

👇 COMMIT BELOW:
Which swap are you making this weekend?
1, 2, or 3? Tell me in the comments.

latestPosts.alt: Photo by Vanessa Hahn on January 03, 2026. May be an image of medicine, condiment, butter, olive oil and text.

latestPosts.caption:
𝑰 𝒊𝒏𝒅𝒖𝒍𝒈𝒆𝒅 𝒐𝒗𝒆𝒓 𝒕𝒉𝒆 𝒉𝒐𝒍𝒊𝒅𝒂𝒚𝒔. 𝑨𝒏𝒅 𝑰 𝒓𝒆𝒇𝒖𝒔𝒆 𝒕𝒐 𝒇𝒆𝒆𝒍 𝒈𝒖𝒊𝒍𝒕𝒚 𝒂𝒃𝒐𝒖𝒕 𝒊𝒕. 🥂

This week, I stepped off-plan.

I enjoyed some Bailey’s with milk and whipped cream with my family. In the moment, I didn't track the sugar or worry about the insulin spike. I just enjoyed the evening.

In my old mindset and maybe yours right now this would have triggered the Cycle of Guilt.

The Old Cycle:

Trigger: Indulge in sugary/alcoholic drinks.

Thought: "I have no willpower. I’ve ruined my metabolic progress."

Feeling: Shame, failure, frustration.

Action: "Well, the week is ruined, I might as well keep eating sugar."

The Hahn Health Approach: We replace Drama with Data. As a Nurse Practitioner, I observed exactly how my body responded over the following 48 hours:

The Morning After: I woke up and the data was clear. My whole body ached, my joints were sore, and my ankle specifically was throbbing. (Data: Sugar and alcohol are immediate inflammatory triggers).

The Scale: I was up 2 lbs. (Data: That isn't fat; it’s systemic water retention from the inflammatory response).

The Lingering Effect: For the next two days, my brain was screaming for more sugar. (Data: The "sugar roller coaster" affecting neurochemistry and insulin levels).

Notice what is missing? Shame is missing.

I am not "bad" for having a drink. My body was simply giving me feedback on the fuel I provided.

The Fix: I didn't starve myself to "make up" for it. I simply went back to my foundation: high protein, extra hydration, and metabolic consistency. The aches subsided, the water weight dropped, and the "data" returned to baseline.

 FRIDAY CONFESSION: What is something you indulged in recently that made you feel "guilty"?

Drop it in the comments. Let’s look at the data, release the shame, and get back to the foundation together.

#MetabolicHealth #DataNotDrama #NursePractitioner #HolidayRecovery #Inflammation #FoodFreedom #HahnHealth #FuelYourFoundation #JointPain #SugarCravings
latestPosts.alt: Photo by Vanessa Hahn on January 02, 2026. May be an image of drink, whipped cream and text that says '8B BAILEYS ORIGIN'.

latestPosts.caption:
𝑰𝒕 𝒊𝒔 𝒉𝒂𝒓𝒅 𝒕𝒐 𝒕𝒉𝒂𝒏𝒌 𝒂 𝒃𝒐𝒅𝒚 𝒕𝒉𝒂𝒕 𝒇𝒆𝒆𝒍𝒔 "𝒃𝒓𝒐𝒌𝒆𝒏." 🩹

Right now, I am navigating life on one leg.
My fibula is fractured. My ligaments are torn.
And to be honest? There are days I want to look at my leg and scream. I want to be angry at it for failing me.

I see this same anger in my patients with Metabolic Syndrome.
You look in the mirror and you feel betrayed.
"𝑾𝒉𝒚 𝒄𝒂𝒏'𝒕 𝑰 𝒍𝒐𝒔𝒆 𝒕𝒉𝒆 𝒘𝒆𝒊𝒈𝒉𝒕?"
"𝑾𝒉𝒚 𝒂𝒎 𝑰 𝒔𝒐 𝒕𝒊𝒓𝒆𝒅?"
"𝑾𝒉𝒚 𝒅𝒐𝒆𝒔𝒏'𝒕 𝒎𝒚 𝒃𝒐𝒅𝒚 𝒘𝒐𝒓𝒌 𝒍𝒊𝒌𝒆 𝒊𝒕 𝒖𝒔𝒆𝒅 𝒕𝒐?"

But here is the biological truth I remind myself of every day:
You cannot heal a body you are at war with.
When you hate your body, you spike Cortisol (the stress hormone).
Cortisol increases inflammation.
Cortisol raises blood sugar.
Cortisol slows down bone healing and metabolic repair.

So, today, I am choosing a different path. I am practicing "Radical Gratitude."
I am not grateful that my leg is broken.
But I am grateful that while my bone is broken, my lungs are breathing perfectly.
I am grateful that my heart is pumping without me asking.
I am grateful that my body is knitting that bone back together, cell by cell, while I sleep.

The Challenge:
If you are frustrated with your health, find one thing your body is doing right today.
Is it digesting your lunch? Is it letting you hug your child? Is it fighting off a cold?

👇 THANK YOU NOTE:
Complete this sentence in the comments:
"Even though I'm frustrated with [X], I am grateful my body can still [Y]."
(My answer: Even though I'm frustrated with my leg, I'm grateful my arms are strong enough to carry me.)

#RadicalGratitude #HealingMindset #CortisolControl #MetabolicSyndrome #FibulaFracture #RecoveryMode #BodyNeutrality #HahnHealth #NursePractitioner #MindBodyConnection
latestPosts.alt: Photo by Vanessa Hahn on January 01, 2026. May be an image of xray, bone and text that says '"Dear Body: Thank νου you for not not giving up on me." 00 hahnhealth.org'.

latestPosts.caption:
𝑺𝒕𝒐𝒑 "𝑺𝒉𝒐𝒖𝒍𝒅-𝒊𝒏𝒈" 𝒐𝒏 𝒚𝒐𝒖𝒓𝒔𝒆𝒍𝒇. 💩

If you are struggling to stick to your health goals, check your vocabulary. How many times a day do you use the word "Should"?
"I should lose weight."
"I should go to the gym."
"I shouldn't have eaten that."

In 𝐂𝐨𝐠𝐧𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐞 𝐁𝐞𝐡𝐚𝐯𝐢𝐨𝐫𝐚𝐥 𝐓𝐡𝐞𝐫𝐚𝐩𝐲 (𝐂𝐁𝐓), we call these "Should Statements." They are cognitive distortions that create guilt, shame, and pressure.

Here is the problem:
"Should" implies you are bad if you don't do it. It creates an internal fight between a Dictator and a Rebel. And guess what? The Rebel usually wins (by procrastinating or overeating).

The Fix (The Semantic Method):
We need to move from Obligation to Desire.
Change your "Should" to an "I Want" or "It would be nice if."
❌ Old Way: "I should go for a walk because I'm lazy." (Guilt-based)
✅ New Way: "I want to go for a walk because it clears my head and lowers my blood sugar." (Value-based)

The Challenge:
Catch yourself saying "should" today. Stop. Take a breath. And rephrase it to remind yourself why you are doing this.

👇 DROP A COMMENT:
What is one thing you "should" do today? Rephrase it below starting with "I WANT to..." and let's see how the energy shifts.

#ShouldStatements #CognitiveDistortions #TeamCBT #DavidBurns #LanguageMatters #MetabolicHealth #MindsetShift #BusinessByDesign #WellnessJourney #HahnHealth

𝒀𝒐𝒖 𝒂𝒓𝒆𝒏'𝒕 "𝒄𝒓𝒂𝒛𝒚." 𝒀𝒐𝒖 𝒎𝒊𝒈𝒉𝒕 𝒋𝒖𝒔𝒕 𝒃𝒆 𝒊𝒏𝒇𝒍𝒂𝒎𝒆𝒅. 🧠🔥

We often treat Mental Health and Metabolic Health as two separate departments.
We go to a therapist for our mood.
We go to a trainer for our weight.
𝐁𝐮𝐭 𝐬𝐜𝐢𝐞𝐧𝐜𝐞 𝐭𝐞𝐥𝐥𝐬 𝐮𝐬 𝐭𝐡𝐞𝐲 𝐚𝐫𝐞 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐬𝐚𝐦𝐞 𝐝𝐞𝐩𝐚𝐫𝐭𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭.

If you struggle with:
❌ Sudden waves of anxiety or panic.
❌ Irritability (being "hangry") 2 hours after a meal.
❌ Brain fog that won't lift.
You might not have a personality problem. You might have a 𝐆𝐥𝐮𝐜𝐨𝐬𝐞 𝐏𝐫𝐨𝐛𝐥𝐞𝐦.

Here is the mechanism:
When you ride the "Glucose Rollercoaster" (high carbs, sugar crashes), your body releases stress hormones (Cortisol and Adrenaline) to regulate your blood sugar.
Those hormones are the exact same ones your body releases when a tiger is chasing you.

So, you’re sitting at your desk, safe and sound, but your body is chemically in "Fight or Flight" mode because of that bagel you ate for breakfast.

The Mindset Shift:
Before you label yourself as "anxious" or "depressed," look at your data.
Are you fueling your brain with stable energy (fats/proteins)? Or are you setting it on fire with sugar spikes?

👇 REAL TALK:
Have you ever noticed a drop in your mood right after a sugar crash? Let’s connect the dots. Drop a comment below.

#NeuroInflammation #MetabolicSyndrome #MentalHealthAwareness #AnxietyRelief #SugarCrash #BrainHealth #NursePractitioner #HahnHealth #ScienceFacts #MoodFood

Sometimes, it's eating protein and healthy fats to stop the rollercoaster. Drop you've ever felt the "Sugar Blues."'.

latestPosts.caption:
𝑻𝒉𝒆 "𝑰 𝑹𝒖𝒊𝒏𝒆𝒅 𝑬𝒗𝒆𝒓𝒚𝒕𝒉𝒊𝒏𝒈" 𝑳𝒊𝒆. 🤥

We are conditioned to think health is a switch. You are either "ON" the wagon or "OFF" the wagon.

As a Nurse Practitioner applying the principles of Business By Design to health, I realize this is terrible management.
Think about it: A CEO doesn't bankrupt the entire company just because one deal fell through. They adjust and keep going.
Yet, with our health, we bankrupt ourselves constantly.
This is what Dr. David Burns calls All-or-Nothing Thinking.
It’s the belief that if you aren't doing it 100% perfectly, you are failing 100% completely.

If you are trying to reverse metabolic syndrome, this mindset is dangerous.
👉 Eating one cookie does not undo a week of walking.
👉 Missing one workout does not erase your progress.
The only thing that ruins your progress is the belief that you have to be perfect to succeed.

The Mindset Shift:
Healing doesn't happen in black and white. It happens in the messy gray areas.
Stop trying to be an "A+" student with your health.
Aim for "B-" work that you can sustain forever.

👇 TELL ME:
Have you fallen into the "All-or-Nothing" trap recently? What was the small slip-up that made you want to throw in the towel?

#CognitiveDistortions #DavidBurns #AllOrNothing #PerfectionismRecovery #MetabolicSyndrome #HealthMindset #ProgressNotPerfection #MondayMotivation #HahnHealth

𝑺𝒕𝒐𝒑 𝒕𝒓𝒚𝒊𝒏𝒈 𝒕𝒐 "𝒇𝒊𝒏𝒅" 𝒂𝒏 𝒉𝒐𝒖𝒓. 𝑺𝒕𝒆𝒂𝒍 5 𝒎𝒊𝒏𝒖𝒕𝒆𝒔. ⏱️

We have an "All-or-Nothing" mindset about fitness (thanks, Dr. Burns, for pointing that out!).
We think: "I’m too busy to go to the gym, so I’ll do nothing."
But as a Nurse Practitioner, I want to introduce you to the concept of "𝑬𝒙𝒆𝒓𝒄𝒊𝒔𝒆 𝑺𝒏𝒂𝒄𝒌𝒊𝒏𝒈."

Research suggests that short bursts of movement (exercise snacks) spread throughout the day can be just as effective for metabolic health as one long session.

Why?
When you move your muscles even just doing overhead presses with water bottles while sitting on the couch your muscles act like sponges. They soak up the glucose (sugar) in your blood stream to use as fuel.
This lowers your insulin. This clears the "fog."

My Current Reality:
I can’t run. I can’t walk for cardio.
So, I am becoming the Queen of the "Arm Workout" between documenting patient charts.

The Challenge:
Do not overcomplicate this.
While your coffee brews? Do 20 squats (or arm circles).
While you brush your teeth? Do calf raises (on your good leg!).
On a Zoom call? Flex your abs.

👇 MOVEMENT CHECK:
What is one "Exercise Snack" you can eat today?
(I’m doing seated overhead presses. Join me!)

#ExerciseSnacks #MetabolicHealth #SeatedWorkout #NWBRecovery #FibulaFracture #NursePractitioner #HahnHealth #SmallSteps #GlucoseSpike #FitnessMyths

𝑻𝒉𝒆 𝑶𝒍𝒚𝒎𝒑𝒊𝒄 𝑺𝒑𝒐𝒓𝒕 𝒏𝒐𝒃𝒐𝒅𝒚 𝒕𝒓𝒂𝒊𝒏𝒔 𝒇𝒐𝒓.🏅

If you need me, I’ll just be here in my kitchen, trying to figure out the physics of getting caffeine into my body without use of my hands or my right leg.

𝑪𝒖𝒓𝒓𝒆𝒏𝒕 𝑺𝒕𝒂𝒕𝒖𝒔: 90% Flamingo, 10% Nurse Practitioner.

Why am I sharing this (besides for your entertainment)?
Because Recovery and Health is messy.
We want the journey to look like a polished montage where we eat the salad and smile.
In reality?
It looks like sliding a coffee mug across a counter because you can’t walk.
It looks like meal prepping while sitting on a stool because your back hurts.
It looks like ordering the burger without the bun and having to explain it to the waiter.
𝑱𝒂𝒎𝒆𝒔 𝑾𝒆𝒅𝒎𝒐𝒓𝒆 𝒔𝒂𝒚𝒔: "We don't need it to be perfect; we just need it to get done."

I didn't look graceful getting my coffee this morning. But I got the coffee.
And that counts as a win.

👇 FRIDAY FUNNY:
What is the most ridiculous thing you’ve had to do to "make it work" this week?
(Bonus points if anyone else has mastered the "Crutch Coffee Carry" technique drop your tips below!)

#FlamingoLife #FibulaFracture #RecoveryHumor #NursePractitioner #RealLife #HahnHealth #FridayVibes #ImproviseAdaptOvercome #CoffeeStruggle

𝑰 𝒕𝒐𝒐𝒌 𝒕𝒉𝒊𝒔 𝒎𝒐𝒎𝒆𝒏𝒕 𝒇𝒐𝒓 𝒈𝒓𝒂𝒏𝒕𝒆𝒅. 🕰️

I found this photo in my camera roll today.
At the time, I remember criticizing myself. I probably thought I looked tired, or I wished I was 5 pounds lighter, or I was stressed about a deadline.
Looking at it now from the perspective of someone recovering from a fractured fibula and torn ligament I just want to shake that woman.

I want to say:
"Do you see that? You are standing. You are walking without pain. Your body is carrying you through the world effortlessly. Stop critiquing it and start thanking it."
We do this with our Metabolic Health, too.
We spend so much time angry at our bodies.
😡 "Why is my metabolism so slow?"
😡 "Why can't I lose the weight faster?"

We treat our bodies like enemies.
But your body is fighting for you every single second. Even when it’s insulin resistant, it is trying to protect you. Even when it’s inflamed, it is trying to heal you.

The Shift:
You cannot heal a body you hate.
Today, I am looking back at this photo not with sadness, but with a promise:
When I am back on two feet, I will never curse my legs for being "too thick" or "too slow" again. I will just be grateful they can move.

👇 THURSDAY GRATITUDE:
Don't wait until you are injured to appreciate what you have.
Name ONE thing your body allows you to do today.

(Can you drive? Can you hug your kids? Can you taste your coffee?)
Tell me below.

#ThrowbackThursday #BodyGratitude #MetabolicHealth #PerspectiveShift #HahnHealth #InjuryRecovery #FibulaFracture #HockeyMom #MindsetMatters
latestPosts.alt: Photo by Vanessa Hahn on December 25, 2025. May be a selfie of bicycle and text.

latestPosts.caption:
𝑻𝒉𝒆 𝒃𝒊𝒍𝒍 𝒇𝒐𝒓 𝒚𝒐𝒖𝒓 𝒄𝒐𝒎𝒇𝒐𝒓𝒕 𝒇𝒐𝒐𝒅 𝒋𝒖𝒔𝒕 𝒂𝒓𝒓𝒊𝒗𝒆𝒅. 🧾

We often look at our habits and ask: "Why can't I stop?"
Dr. David Burns teaches us a different question: "What is the Cost-Benefit Analysis?"
Last week (Day 3), we admitted the Benefits: Sugar soothes us. It’s reliable. It’s quick comfort.

But as the CEO of your own health, you have to look at the Costs.
If we continue to use food to treat our feelings, we have to pay the invoice.
When I look at Metabolic Syndrome, the "price tag" for that nightly sugar hit usually includes:
❌ The Physical Cost: Inflammation that makes my broken leg hurt more.
❌ The Energy Cost: That heavy, sluggish feeling that hits 20 minutes after the sugar high.
❌ The Mental Cost: The shame cycle that starts the moment the plate is empty.

The Paradox:
The thing that comforts you is also the thing hurting you.
I’m not telling you to diet today. I am inviting you to do a simple audit.

👇 THE QUESTION:
Look at your current struggle. Is the "comfort" you get from the food worth the "price" you are paying in your health?
If the price is getting too high, drop a "💸" below.

#CostBenefitAnalysis #MetabolicHealth #TeamCBT #InsulinResistance #HealthMindset #HardTruths #NursePractitioner #HahnHealth #Inflation

𝒀𝒐𝒖𝒓 𝒎𝒆𝒕𝒂𝒃𝒐𝒍𝒊𝒔𝒎 𝒊𝒔𝒏'𝒕 𝒃𝒓𝒐𝒌𝒆𝒏. 𝑻𝒉𝒆 𝒍𝒐𝒄𝒌 𝒊𝒔 𝒋𝒖𝒔𝒕 𝒋𝒂𝒎𝒎𝒆𝒅. 🔐

If you constantly struggle with:
Mid-afternoon fatigue (The Crash)
Cravings for sweets right after eating
Weight accumulating around your belly

You do not have a willpower problem. You likely have Insulin Resistance.

Let’s break down the science simply:

Think of your body’s cells like a house.
To keep the lights on and the house warm, you need fuel (Glucose/Food) to get inside.
Insulin is the KEY that opens the front door to let that fuel in.

✅ In a healthy metabolism:
You eat ➡️ Insulin unlocks the door ➡️ Fuel goes in ➡️ You feel energized.

❌ In Insulin Resistance:
You’ve been ringing the doorbell so many times (eating sugar/frequent snacking) that the lock has become "jammed" or resistant.

You eat ➡️ Insulin tries to use the key, but the lock is stuck ➡️ Fuel stays stuck in your blood (High Blood Sugar) ➡️ Your cells starve.

The Result?
Your blood is full of sugar (toxic), but your cells are starving for energy (fatigue).
So your brain screams: "EAT MORE SUGAR!" trying to get energy in.
It’s a vicious cycle. You aren't greedy. Your cells are starving because the door is stuck.

The Fix:
We have to clean out the lock. This happens when we lower insulin levels (through fasting, low-carb nutrition, or movement) to let the sensitivity return.

👇 CHECK IN:
Does this analogy make sense? Drop a "🔑" if you’ve ever felt like you just ate but your body is still screaming for energy.

#InsulinResistance #MetabolicSyndrome #ScienceFacts #NursePractitioner #HahnHealth #Fatigue #BloodSugar #TheScienceOfWeightLoss #EducationDay
latestPosts.alt: Photo by Vanessa Hahn on December 23, 2025. May be an image of poster, door and text that says 'HAHN HEALTH Energy In EnergyBlocked Energy Blocked hahnhealth.org'.

latestPosts.caption:
𝑻𝒉𝒆 "𝑳𝒂𝒔𝒕 𝑺𝒖𝒑𝒑𝒆𝒓" 𝑺𝒚𝒏𝒅𝒓𝒐𝒎𝒆. 🍽️

How many times have you eaten way too much on a Sunday night because "The Diet Starts Monday"?

We treat Monday like a judge who is going to sentence us to prison (a diet). So, we treat the weekend like a frantic party before the jail cell closes.

This is a trap.

As a Nurse Practitioner, I see the biological cost of this mindset.
Those Sunday binges spike your insulin and inflammation so high that by Monday morning, you are chemically craving more sugar. You are setting yourself up to fail before the week even begins.
The "Business By Design" Mindset Shift:
A CEO doesn't wait for "next week" to fix a bleeding bank account. They fix it the moment they see it.
Your metabolic health is the same.
There is no magic in Monday.
There is no glory in a "perfect start."
The Antidote:
The best time to reset your metabolism was 10 years ago.
The second best time is your very next bite.
Not tomorrow. Not Monday. Next bite.

👇 COMMITMENT:
Drop the word "NOW" below if you are willing to break the "Monday" cycle and just make a good choice today.

#MondayMotivation #StartNow #MetabolicSyndrome #LastSupperSyndrome #BingeEatingRecovery #JamesWedmore #HahnHealth #InsulinResistance #MindsetShift

تغذية من أجل قلب صحي 🫀

قلبك يعمل بلا توقف ليحافظ على أداء جسمك بأفضل حال. دعم صحة القلب من خلال التغذية السليمة يساعد على الحفاظ على دورة دموية صحية، توازن مستويات الكوليسترول، وصحة القلب على المدى الطويل.

هذا المحتوى يسلّط الضوء على أطعمة طبيعية كاملة تدعم صحة القلب، غنية بالألياف، مضادات الأكسدة، والدهون الصحية التي تساهم في حماية القلب وتعزيز الحيوية العامة.

كيف تدعم هذه الأطعمة صحة القلب؟

الشوفان غني بالألياف القابلة للذوبان التي تساعد على تنظيم الكوليسترول
الأسماك الدهنية توفّر أوميغا 3 التي تدعم انتظام ضربات القلب والدورة الدموية
السبانخ تحتوي على عناصر غذائية تدعم صحة الأوعية الدموية
الطماطم غنية بالليكوبين الذي يساهم في حماية القلب
المكسرات توفّر دهونًا صحية تدعم وظيفة القلب
الأفوكادو وزيت الزيتون يحتويان على دهون أحادية مفيدة لصحة الشرايين
الثوم يحتوي على مركبات كبريتية تساعد على تحسين الدورة الدموية
التوت غني بمضادات الأكسدة التي تقلل من الإجهاد التأكسدي

✨ القلب الصحي يبدأ باختياراتنا اليومية.
اختيار الأطعمة الكاملة والغنية بالعناصر الغذائية هو خطوة بسيطة لكنها مؤثرة لدعم قوة القلب، تحسين الدورة الدموية، والحفاظ على صحة القلب على المدى البعيد

اللهم ارزقنا فرحة لا تسعها الدنيا ومن عليها🍃🩵

#اكسبلور #photooftheday #iraq #ms #mood #coffee #baghdad #2026 #reel

دماغك يحتاج الوقود الصحيح ليدعم الذاكرة، التركيز، صفاء الذهن وصحتك الإدراكية على المدى البعيد.
اختياراتك اليومية في الأكل تلعب دور كبير في طريقة عمل الدماغ اليوم وكيف يتغير مع الزمن.

هذه الأطعمة غنية بالمغذيات وتدعم صحة الدماغ بشكل طبيعي:

•التوت الأزرق: مضادات أكسدة تحمي خلايا الدماغ من الإجهاد التأكسدي
•الجوز: دهون صحية تدعم الذاكرة وإشارات الدماغ
•الشوكولاتة الداكنة: فلافونيدات تساعد على تدفق الدم للدماغ
•سمك السالمون: أوميغا-3 مهمة لوظائف الدماغ والبنية العصبية
•بذور اليقطين: مغنيسيوم يدعم التواصل العصبي
•الخضروات الورقية: عناصر غذائية مهمة للأداء الذهني
•البيض: الكولين… مهم للذاكرة وصناعة النواقل العصبية
•الأفوكادو: دهون صحية تدعم تدفق الدم وتغذية الدماغ

✨ صحتك العقلية تبدأ من طبقك قبل أي شيء.
اختاروا طعامًا حقيقيًا… ليمنحكم تركيزًا أوضح، ذاكرة أقوى، وراحة ذهنية على المدى البعيد

latestPosts.caption:
WHAT IS MOBILE PERSONAL TRAINING?

Mobile personal training means fitness and nutrition brought directly to YOU! 🎯 Whether it’s your home, condo gym, or workplace, we provide:

✔️ Personalized workouts tailored to your goals
✔️ Customized nutrition plans for your lifestyle
✔️ 1:1 coaching, partner training, and small group sessions
✔️ All the equipment, guidance, and motivation you need

💡 Specializing in peri to post-menopause fitness and nutrition, we offer tailored support to meet you exactly where you are in your health journey.

No commutes. No crowded gyms. Just convenient coaching designed to fit your life! 💪

Making Harrow and surrounding towns healthier!

📩 Message us to learn more or book your 15-minute consultation! Let’s ignite your health goals together! 🔥

That’s where coaching matters.

As a GLP-1 Lifestyle Integration Coach, I help women:

✔ Maintain muscle while losing fat
✔ Support metabolism during hormonal changes
✔ Manage low energy & appetite changes
✔ Avoid rebound weight gain

latestPosts.caption:
Using a GLP-1 or thinking about it during peri or post-menopause?
Your lifestyle still matters — now more than ever.

As a GLP-1 Lifestyle Integration Coach, I help women navigate weight loss during midlife by pairing nutrition, strength training, and sustainable habits with their GLP-1 journey.

✨ We focus on:
• Eating enough protein to protect muscle
• Strength training to support metabolism
• Managing fatigue, digestion & recovery
• Building habits that last — on or off medication

Weight loss isn’t just about the scale.
It’s about feeling strong, supported, and confident through every phase.

latestPosts.caption:
Bone health is one of the most overlooked parts of women’s wellness — until it becomes a problem. The truth is, bone loss doesn’t happen overnight. It’s gradual, silent, and often unnoticed… but it is highly preventable with the right habits.

💫 Why Women Are More at Risk
Women experience bone loss earlier and more rapidly than men, especially during:

🔹Perimenopause (when estrogen begins to fluctuate)
🔹Menopause (when estrogen declines sharply)
🔹Post-menopause (when bone turnover increases)

Estrogen plays a major role in keeping bones dense and strong. When levels drop, bone resorption speeds up — meaning your body breaks down bone faster than it rebuilds it. 

This is why women can lose up to 20% of bone density in the first 5–7 years of menopause.

#osteoporosis #bonehealth #liftweights #weightlifting #fitover50 #fitover60 #startnow #houstonfit #houston #personaltrainer #women #menopause #estrogen

If you want to lose at least 30 lbs by June and you’re over 40, this matters.

Weight loss at this stage of life isn’t about trying harder.
It’s about doing the right things consistently.

You don’t need to cut carbs.
You don’t need exhausting workouts.
And you don’t need to track every calorie you eat.

What actually works is simple:
• Meals built around protein and fiber
• Protecting your sleep (this is huge for hormones)
• Staying consistent instead of starting over every Monday

This is how I coach women over 40 to lose weight without extremes — and keep it off.

If this feels different from what you’ve tried before, that’s because it is.

Save this and come back to it when motivation dips.
Consistency is the real secret.

After the holidays, it’s normal to feel heavier, bloated, or “off.”

That doesn’t mean you gained body fat overnight.
Most of what you’re seeing is water retention from higher carbs, sodium, and disrupted routines.

This 5-day reset is about getting back to basics
hydration
fiber
protein
daily movement
simple meals

No extremes.
No detoxes.
No starting over.

Just consistent habits that help your body release water, feel lighter, and get back into a fat-loss rhythm.

#WomenOver40 #FatLossAfter40 #PostHolidayReset #PerimenopauseSupport #MenopauseHealth SustainableFatLoss

Fat loss should not feel this hard… but for many women, it does.

If you’re eating “healthy,” doing cardio, and still frustrated with the scale, it’s usually not because you’re failing — it’s because a few basics are missing.

The most common things I see
• not enough protein
• too much cardio, not enough strength training
• hidden calories from drinks and cooking habits

And yes hormones, sleep, and stress matter.
But when these three aren’t in place, fat loss will always feel like an uphill battle.

The good news
You don’t need extreme dieting or more workouts.
You need the right habits for this stage of life.

Save this for later and follow for simple fat loss tips for women over 40.

Coach Edribui

#WomenOver40 #FatLossForWomen #PerimenopauseSupport #MenopauseHealth #ProteinForWomen StrengthTrainingForWomen

Doing everything right during the day but still struggling to lose stubborn fat?

For women over 40, hormonal shifts and a slower metabolism make your evening habits more important than ever. Just 1–2 simple mistakes at night can switch your body from fat-burning mode to fat-storing mode.

Here are 5 easy, hormone-friendly habits to help you burn more fat while you sleep:

1️⃣ Keep Dinner Light & Protein-Focused
Stick to a palm-sized portion of lean protein (like salmon, chicken, or tofu) with half your plate full of veggies. Avoid processed carbs and heavy meals that spike insulin.

2️⃣ Eat 2–3 Hours Before Bed
Late-night eating can disrupt sleep and fat metabolism. If you’re hungry, choose a light, protein-rich snack not sugar or carbs.

3️⃣ Cut Off Blue Light 1 Hour Before Bed
Phone down, TV off! Blue light blocks melatonin, and quality sleep is key for fat loss  especially for women over 40.

4️⃣ Kitchen Closed After Dinner
Clean up, dim the lights, and brush your teeth early to signal you’re done eating. This helps reduce late-night snacking.

5️⃣ Move a Little After Dinner
Take a 10-minute walk or stretch before bed. It helps with digestion, lowers blood sugar, and supports overnight fat burning.

👉🏾Tiny night-time changes = BIG results over time especially for women over 40 working to balance hormones and boost fat loss.

#FatLossForWomenOver40 #WomenOver40 #NightRoutine #FatBurningTips #HormoneHealth MetabolismBoost

You’re not crazy
You’re not lazy
You’re probably in perimenopause

And no it doesn’t always start with hot flushes or missed periods
For many women the first sign is this
I just don’t feel like myself

It can hit in your mid 30s earlier than most of us expect
Even when nothing’s changed in your life suddenly you’re dealing with
😴 Fatigue that won’t budge
😠 Mood swings or irritability
☁️ Brain fog
😟 Anxiety out of nowhere

These show up before the physical signs like stubborn belly fat
Because your hormones are shifting silently but powerfully

The more you understand what’s happening
The better you can support your body through it
It’s not about doing more
It’s about doing things differently
Especially with food and lifestyle

And the best part
With the right tweaks
You can feel like you again

Coach Ed Ribui 💚

#PerimenopauseSupport
#WomensHealthOver40
#FeelLikeYouAgain
#PerimenopauseSymptoms
#TiredOfFeelingTired

I have a different approach when it comes to the holidays and it’s one that actually helps my clients enjoy them.

I don’t recommend fasting or skipping meals. That usually leads to extreme hunger, overeating later, feeling bloated, and not fully enjoying the moment.

Instead, I suggest one of two simple options before a holiday meal:
2 small snacks
or 1 balanced meal and 1 snack

Prioritize protein and vegetables.
Protein keeps you full.
Vegetables add volume and help with digestion.

When it’s time to eat, don’t restrict.
Include some protein and vegetables, then choose the carbs you truly enjoy and eat them guilt free.

The holidays are about family, connection, and being present not food rules.

And remember,
It’s not what you eat between Christmas and New Year’s it’s what you do between New Year’s and Christmas.

Wishing you and your family a Merry Christmas 🎄
Coach Edribui

You’ve been told fat loss is just about eating less.
So you cut portions. You skip meals. You push harder.

And after 40, your body pushes back.

A calorie deficit simply means your body is using more energy than it takes in.
It does not mean starving yourself or living on tiny meals.

The problem is not that you are doing everything wrong.
The problem is that dieting does not account for hormonal changes in perimenopause and menopause.

After 40, fat loss comes from structure, not restriction.
Balanced meals. Enough protein and fiber. Sleep. Movement. Lower stress.

When those habits are in place, your body naturally enters a calorie deficit and fat loss feels easier instead of exhausting.

If you are tired of dieting and ready for a smarter approach, this is for you.

latestPosts.caption:
Run or walk?
Both move the body.
Only one can truly move with you for life.

Running is powerful.
It builds cardiovascular strength, resilience, discipline.
It challenges the nervous system and sharpens the mind.
But it’s also higher impact — joints, hormones, recovery all need respect.
It’s a season, not always a lifetime.

Walking is the ultimate longevity practice.
Low impact. Sustainable. Regenerative.
It supports circulation, metabolism, lymphatic flow, digestion, and mental clarity.
You can walk every day.
You can walk at any age.
You can walk forever.

Longevity isn’t about pushing harder.
It’s about choosing what you can sustain.

Some days you run.
Every day, you walk.

Health is the destination.

 #FrancescaTheMethod #WalkForLife #HealthyAging #SustainableFitness #MoveDaily WellnessLifestyle

latestIgtvVideos.title: Day 24 of 30 Day Record Yourself Challenge
latestIgtvVideos.caption:
Todays topic is to say a tongue twister and explain it.

Mistakes below negatives 2-4.

十不是十四。是十不是四。3）十不是四十。是十四不是四十 4）四十不是十。是四十不是十四。

In the tongue twister I used their are four sets of positives and four sets of negatives. Basically in Chinese they have words with the same spelling but with a different pronunciation. 

So I believe Chinese from certain provinces pronounce 10 as 4. 10 is s.h.i 3 letters and 4 is s.i. two letters. They omit the "h" for some reason when it shouldn't be omitted. 

In the video I mention people from Sichuan province who say 4 when they mean 10.

Short story: My former co-worker who is my interpreter English to Chinese and Chinese to English when she teaches one on one. 

Sometimes pronounces words incorrectly and I tell her sorry to interrupt. The word is pronounced ________you pronounced it ______. 

She replied and said that's what I said. Knowing that I'm right and she's wrong. I apologize to let her feel proud of herself. 

Then I voice record her teaching the student, and again she pronounces the word incorrectly. 

So I stop the recording and interrupt her again and say listen to this recording. Now she feels embarrassed. 

In this situation we were at a cubicle. And I was taking a break.

In China, people interested in learning English, will misundstand you and dislike you because of it. 

Or in my case say I'm wrong and she's right. Its best as a teacher to proof you're right by secretly recording them. Or else it'll spread nationwide. 

Another example is the English letters should be written in a correct way and Chinese English teachers in public schools teach their students differently. 

I'm not talking about the stroke order I'm talking about adding lines to letters. If you have any Chinese English students ask them to write the alphabet, and you'll see what I mean.

 If you know anything about Chinese culture they like to talk loud infront of you.

 Regardless if you use hearing aids. 
I like to study and film in a quiet place.

Mistakes below 

Not guo T3 its guo T4, Not Ji T1 its Ju T4, not fufa T3 T3, its yufa T3 T3.
