Michael Brandt tells Lance Armstrong to “stack carbs and ketones.”But ketosis only happens when carbs are gone. You can’t have both. Dr. McDougall warned about this kind of marketing years ago.

#health #fitness #nutritionmyth #ketodietskeptic #sportsnutrition #ketoneiq #runningfuel #wholefoodplantbased #drmcdougall #carbloading #enduranceathlete #lowcarbmyth #scienceoversupplements #faddiets #truthaboutketo

Ketone IQ calls ketosis the “best kept secret of energy.”
Dr. McDougall calls it what it is — starvation mode.
Real athletes fuel on carbs, not ketone shots.

1.	“Scientifically proven to improve focus.”
	
•	What Brandt is claiming: that consuming exogenous ketones (via Ketone IQ) leads to improved cognitive focus.
	
•	While ketones are used in specific medical contexts (e.g., epilepsy) the notion that achieving ketosis (or taking ketone supplements) boosts “focus” in healthy people is unproven and questionable. For example, Dr McDougall's article on “Keto versus Starch-based Diets” states:
“Ketosis happens when the body is starving to death … which isn’t a sustainable state to be in.” 
He would challenge the “scientifically proven” language by noting that many claims for low-carb/ketogenic regimes lack long-term human trials and robust cognitive outcomes.
	
•	Research to check: I did not find high-quality large human trials showing that ketone supplements reliably improve focus in healthy people. A general review of ketogenic diets states:
“There is no human studies to support recommending ketosis to treat these (brain) conditions.” 
Brandt’s claim is premature and marketing-oriented rather than firmly evidence-based.

#health #fitness #runningfuel #carbloading #ketodietskeptic #nutritionmyth #ketosis #sportsnutrition #ketoneiq #exogenousketones #faddiets #highperformancefuel #wholefoodplantbased #drmcdougall #enduranceathlete

Is Oil Healthy?
Swipe left to find out! 🌿🛢️
Link in bio.

Top Countries by Vegetable Oil Consumption (per capita, kg/year)

Based on the most recent data:
	1.	United States – ~38.27 kg ￼
	2.	Malaysia – ~30.39 kg ￼
	3.	United Kingdom – ~26.13 kg ￼
	4.	Saudi Arabia – ~23.41 kg ￼
	5.	Japan – ~19.94 kg ￼
	6.	Kazakhstan – ~18.87 kg ￼
	7.	Chile – ~12.16 kg ￼
	8.	Peru – ~11.67 kg ￼
	9.	Vietnam – ~2.97 kg ￼

These reflect per-capita vegetable oil consumption—useful for understanding dietary trends globally.

Explaining Fats to your child: 

Why Oils (Even the “Healthy” Ones) Are Bad for Your Heart
	•	Saturated oils (like coconut, palm, butter)
These are heavy and cloggy fats.
They make your blood vessels stiff and sticky, kind of like putting glue in your pipes. This makes it harder for your heart to pump blood.
	•	Monounsaturated oils (like olive, avocado, canola)
People say these are “good,” but they still hurt the tiny lining inside your blood vessels (the endothelium).
It’s like scratching the inside of a straw—after a while, stuff starts sticking and blocking it.
	•	Polyunsaturated oils (like soybean, sunflower, corn)
These can create inflammation and tiny injuries inside your blood vessels.
Think of them as little sparks that keep burning the walls of your heart’s pipes.
	•	Linoleic acid (found in many seed and vegetable oils)
Your body needs a tiny bit, but too much is like having too much glue—it gums up the works.
Modern diets have way too much, and it makes your arteries weaker and more inflamed.

Esselstyn’s Core Message

“It’s the oil, not the cholesterol in food, that keeps damaging your arteries.”

	•	Oil (any oil, even olive or coconut) is processed, stripped of fiber, and 100% fat.
	•	It narrows blood vessels within minutes of eating it, making it harder for blood to flow.
	•	People with heart disease can’t afford even a little damage—he calls for zero added oil.

#IsOilHealthy #CookingOils #ProcessedFoods #HealthyFats #OilDebate #PlantBasedLiving #oilforkids

latestPosts.caption:
😩 Oh no… not another January.

Another promise to yourself.
Another “this year I’ll finally lose the weight, get fitter, feel comfortable in my own body again.”

You’ve tried before, haven’t you?
	🥗 Starved yourself
	🏃‍♂️ Done endless cardio
	📏 Counted calories till your head hurt
	🥤 Jumped from diet to diet, shake to shake, plan to plan.

And maybe… for a while, it worked.
But then life happened. The weight came back. Again. And all that effort felt like it was for nothing.

So now it’s 2026, and you’re staring down another “New Year, New Me,” wondering why it always ends the same way:
😔 Frustrated.
😤 Exhausted.
💔 Defeated.

Here’s the truth: it’s not your fault.

It’s not laziness. It’s not lack of willpower.
It’s that no one ever explained how your body really works, especially as we get older.
	🧠 How stress silently keeps fat locked onto your body.
	🍔 How certain foods make it impossible to shift stubborn fat
	⚡ Why doing “more” can actually make things worse.
	❌ Why punishing yourself never works.

When you finally understand that, fat loss stops feeling like a fight.
It starts to make sense. ✅

I’ve seen it happen with people just like you:
🏆 Julie  6 stone down
🏆 Geoff 5 stone down
🏆 Steve 9 stone down
🏆 Sandy 2 stone down

Not through extremes. Not by punishment.
Through smarter, realistic approaches that actually stick.

This year doesn’t need more guilt.
It doesn’t need another broken promise.
It needs clarity, understanding, and a real way forward.

#NewYearNewMe #WeightLossJourney #HealthyLiving #GetFit #Wellness

latestPosts.caption:
If You’re in Your 40s, 50s or 60s and Not Exercising, This Should Concern You.

For many professionals, the day follows a familiar pattern.

Sitting in the car.
Sitting at a desk.
Sitting in meetings.
Staring at a screen for hours.

Then finishing the day sitting at home, scrolling on a phone or watching television.

Weeks turn into months. Months turn into years. And the body is barely used.

The issue is not lack of intelligence, discipline or success. It’s the slow accumulation of habits that quietly undermine health.

From your 40s onwards, muscle is no longer something the body protects. If it’s not used, it’s lost. And it’s lost far quicker than most people realise.

When muscle mass declines, several things happen at once. Metabolism slows. Body fat increases. Strength and balance reduce. Joints feel stiffer. Everyday tasks require more effort. Energy levels drop.

This isn’t ageing it’s inactivity.

What often follows is a predictable cycle. Long working days lead to fatigue, fatigue leads to convenience eating, convenience eating leads to weight gain and declining confidence. Cooking real food feels like effort. Exercise feels like something you’ll “get back to” later.

Later rarely comes.

The uncomfortable truth is that doing nothing does not keep things the same. It makes things worse slowly, quietly and consistently.

The good news is that this decline is not inevitable.

When the body starts moving again, energy improves. When energy improves, food choices improve. Momentum returns.

This is not about vanity. It’s about capability, resilience and longevity being able to think clearly, move well and feel physically confident in your 50s, 60s and beyond.

As the new year approaches, it may be time to question habits that no longer serve you.

Sitting all day.
Scrolling all evening.
Relying on convenience instead of care.

These habits are not harmless.

Perhaps this is the year to change them.

Not through extremes or guilt but through consistent, intentional action.

Because the cost of continuing as you are will always be higher than the effort it takes to change.

And there is no better time than now.
#muscle #40 #weight #health

latestPosts.caption:
✨ “I Finally Feel Like Me Again.”Sandy’s Story. 
From Three Pounds in Three Years… to Two Stone Lost ✨🚨💪🏻👖

Some stories deserve to be told.

This amazing woman, Sandy, is in her late 50s.
And like so many women her age, her life hasn’t been simple.

She’s spent years looking after everyone else… and putting herself last.
Kids, grandkids, work, family, responsibilities always on her shoulders.

Meanwhile, her own body changed in ways she didn’t recognise.
Slower metabolism.
Weight that wouldn’t budge.
Hormones all over the place.
Aches, pains, tiredness that never seemed to disappear.

She told me:

Getting dressed became one of the hardest parts of her day. Her clothes didn’t sit properly, and she didn’t feel confident in anything she put on. Her energy was low, and she felt like her body was ageing faster than she expected.

For decades, Sandy tried everything to lose weight.

The most she had ever lost was three pounds in three years.

Then came health scares, illnesses, and moments where she wondered if this was just “how life was going to be now.”

But something inside her refused to give up.

This year, Sandy changed her story.

She has done incredible well. 

🔥 She has now lost 2 stone.
🔥 She has dropped 4 inches from her waist and 4 inches from her hips.
🔥 She has lost nearly 10% body fat.
🔥 And she’s seeing a number on the scales she hasn’t seen in over ten years.

But the numbers aren’t even the best part.

What she says now is:

“I feel lighter in every way.
I’ve got energy again.
I can move easily.
I feel stronger.
My clothes fit me.
I actually like what I see in the mirror.
I feel proud of myself… and that feels amazing.”

She’s over the moon with what she’s achieved.
She’s happier, healthier, and more confident than she’s felt in a decade.

Sandy’s story is for every woman who’s in her 50s thinking:

“I’ve tried everything.”
“It’s too late for me.”
“I don’t feel like myself anymore.”

It’s not too late.
You’re not stuck.
You can feel amazing again inside and out.

Sandy is living proof.

Well done Sandy do proud of YOU!💪🏻 #weightlossjourney #weightlifting #healthfood #happiness

“My doctor says I’m fine now.”

Let’s be honest though - you’re not fine.

The tablets are managing the symptom, but not fixing the problem. High blood pressure builds quietly over years of stress, poor sleep, and other contributing factors. And unfortunately pills can’t undo that. I’ve seen clients in their 40s, 50s, and 60s turn their health around — losing weight, normalising blood pressure, and even reducing or completely coming off their medication all together.

⚠️ Don’t wait for a heart attack to force you into change.

❤️ Start today. Stop relying on tablets. Start taking control.

If you’re ready to make a real, lasting change but don’t know where to start - that’s where we can help.
I’ll guide you step by step with the right training, nutrition, and support to help you lower your blood pressure naturally. But also so you feel stronger and take back control of your health for good.

My DM's are always open so feel free to drop me a message. Or you can email: richardtaylorptc@gmail.com

Do you ever look in the mirror and wonder… who is this? 🪞🤔
Do you feel like your energy has disappeared, or that you’re relying on painkillers just to get through the day? 💊😔

Dave joined my 12-week program hoping to lose 1 stone… and he’s already smashed it, losing over 2 stone ⚡ and more than 5–6 inches from his waist 📏! Phase one is done, and the results are phenomenal 🌟.

Now, he’s onto Phase Two – the fun part 🎉: sculpting, toning, and building long-lasting definition 💥. This is all about muscle tone that lasts, not just a quick fix 🏋️‍♂️.

latestPosts.caption:
No One’s Coming to Save You

Let’s be honest. No one is coming to discover you.
No one is coming to knock on your door and hand you a better life.

If you’re waiting for the right time when you feel motivated, when life gets easier, when things “settle down” you’ll be waiting forever.

I’ve seen it time and time again, with people who are smart, kind, hard-working… but stuck. They want change. They talk about change. But they never take that first step because they think they have to feel ready before they begin.

Here’s the truth: you won’t feel ready.

You’ll feel uncertain. You’ll feel tired. You’ll feel like you’re starting too late, or that you’ve failed too many times before. But one day, something inside you says: enough.

That’s the day that changes everything. Not the day you join a gym, or start a diet, or buy a new pair of trainers. The day you decide that your past doesn’t define you and you refuse to stay where you are.

You take one small step forward. You go for that short walk. You make one better meal choice. You drink that glass of water instead of wine. It doesn’t feel like much. But that’s how change begins - quietly, imperfectly, one step at a time.

There’s nothing you can do about the past. It’s gone. What matters is what you do next.

Because every single day, you have two choices: You can either stay where you are or you can move slowly towards who you want to become. And when you start doing that even when you don’t feel good, even when no one’s cheering you on, something shifts inside you. 

No one’s coming to save you. But the moment you realise that, is the moment you finally start saving yourself!

🔥 WHY WAITING TO “GET HEALTHY” IS STEALING YOUR LIFE 🔥

Most people think getting healthy is just about losing a bit of weight… or fitting into old clothes.
It’s not. It’s about life itself.

💪 Stronger = unstoppable
🏃 Leaner = confident
✨ Fitter = add quality years to life

The sad truth? Most wait until it’s urgent:
	•	When aches and pains arrive
	•	When climbing stairs feels like a workout
	•	When energy crashes and simple tasks feel like effort
	•	When the doctor warns about blood pressure, cholesterol, or weight

By then, years of vitality are already lost.

Yes, it’s hard. Yes, it takes effort. Yes, you’ll feel like giving up.

But here’s the truth: if you don’t invest in your health now, who will?
❌ Not your boss
❌ Not your friends
❌ Not even your family

When you finally decide enough is enough, life opens up:
💥 You feel stronger
💥 You feel happier
💥 You feel alive again

Investing in your health isn’t a luxury.
It’s the most important investment you’ll ever make.

🔥 So… how long are you going to keep waiting? 🔥

#HealthIsWealth #Longevity #WeightLossJourney #StrongerEveryDay #MidlifeFitness #InvestInYourself #HealthyLifestyle #FitnessMotivation #Over40Fitness #TransformYourLife

latestPosts.caption:
Why We Quit Too Soon and How to Push Through.

Have you ever started a weight-loss or fitness journey, felt excited in the beginning, and then… stopped?
👉 Maybe the scale didn’t move fast enough.
👉 Maybe you felt tired and wondered if it was worth it.
👉 Maybe you thought, “This just isn’t working for me.”

This is one of the biggest reasons people give up they expect instant results.
But here’s the truth:
👉 Fat loss takes time.
👉 Building strength takes consistency.
👉 Improving your health is a lifelong investment not a two-week fix.

Why the Results Aren’t Showing (Yet)

When you start eating better, moving more, and looking after yourself, your body is changing even if you can’t see it straight away.
👉 Your metabolism is adjusting.
👉 Your energy systems are adapting.
👉 Your body is repairing damage that’s been years in the making.

But because we live in a world of quick fixes, if the results aren’t visible in the mirror or on the scale within a few weeks, we convince ourselves nothing is happening and quit.

The Real Progress
👉 You’re sleeping better
👉 Your cravings are calming down
👉 You’re getting stronger and fitter
👉 Your confidence is growing

These are all signs of progress that lead to bigger changes if you stick with it.

The Message? Don’t Quit.

The people who get results are the ones who push through the weeks where nothing seems to be happening.
Because results don’t come to the quickest they come to the most consistent.

So next time you feel like giving up, remind yourself:
Progress is happening, even when you can’t see it yet. Keep going. Your future self will thank you.

#WeightLossJourney #HealthAndWellbeing #KeepGoing #Motivation #FitnessForLife #HealthyLifestyle #ConsistencyIsKey #MindsetMatters #WellnessJourney #ProgressNotPerfection

latestPosts.caption:
🎈🎂💪🏽💚 EIGHTEEN YEARS STRONG!!!

Today we celebrate 18 years in business at The Fitness Unit, and what an incredible journey it has been. From day one to now, we are so grateful to every single person who has walked through our doors - whether you’ve been a member or trainer in the past, trained with us in the old place, or are one of our amazing current clients. And remember… our door is always open 💚

Over the years, we are incredibly proud to have built our Private Personal Training Studio, where every client is treated as an individual… whether it’s 1:1 PT, Semi-Private, or Small Group Training. We also run our most supported Body Transformation Program, alongside mentoring for those ready to commit to a complete lifestyle change.

As we continue to grow and evolve, we’re so excited to have expanded into The Flow Studio, a space dedicated to yoga, Pilates. We’ve had some amazing retreat days, weekends away, team-building and social events, and so much more. This marks a huge chapter for us, as we continue to build a community that supports both physical and mental wellbeing. We truly are more than just a gym - we are a place to move, connect, reset, and grow 💚

What truly makes The Fitness Unit so special is our incredible team of professional, supportive, and passionate trainers. We are beyond grateful for a team that genuinely cares, brings energy and expertise every single day, and shares our vision of changing lives… not just physically, but mentally too. We couldn’t do this without them 💪🏽💚

Eighteen years on, we still feel incredibly lucky to do what we do, work genuinely does not feel like work. It is an honour to help so many people transform their lives, improve their health and fitness, and achieve goals they once thought were impossible.

Here’s to many more years of strength, growth, connection, and community 🥂💪🏽

🚨 Final call for our BODY TRANSFORMATION PROGRAM DM us to get started!

Thank you for all your continued support over the years.

With so much love and gratitude,
Anita & Rachel Bond xx
Owners @fitnessunit_ipswich 💚

latestPosts.caption:
Is dried fruit really a wholesome snack… or just candy in disguise? 🍬🍑

As a Board Certified Functional Medicine Practitioner, degreed and experienced in nursing and mental health & addictions counseling (including food addiction), and a Certified Lifestyle Weight Management Specialist, Certified Specialist in Menopause Coaching, and Certified GLP-1 Exercise Specialist, I’m often asked if dried fruit fits into a “healthy” lifestyle.

Let’s break it down 👇

🟣 Sugar Content
Dried fruit looks like a smaller version of fresh fruit, but don’t be fooled. Drying concentrates sugar.  That can lead to accidental overconsumption, especially if you're managing weight, blood sugar, or cravings.

🔵 Fiber
Yes, fiber stays intact—sometimes even more per serving than fresh. But fiber doesn’t cancel out sugar, especially when metabolic markers like fasting insulin, blood sugar, or HA1C are elevated.

🟡 Nutrient Loss
Drying destroys vitamin C, so dried fruit isn’t as nutrient-dense. Some minerals stay, but it’s not a complete trade.

🟠 Dental Health
Sticky + sugary = a cavity risk. Dried fruit clings to teeth, so brushing after is smart. 🪥

🔴 Context Matters
Dried fruit isn’t “bad,” but it's easy to overdo it, especially if you're:

➡️ Managing insulin resistance or diabetes
➡️ Trying to lose fat
➡️ Navigating food addiction
➡️ In peri- or post-menopause
➡️ Sedentary and not burning quick carbs

💡 Bottom line:  Fresh fruit 🍎🍓 offers hydration, fiber, and better fullness cues.

✨ Real food is powerful. Context is king. Don’t follow trends—follow your body and the science.

💬 Do you eat dried fruit regularly? 

❓Ever eaten more than you meant to? 

Let’s talk ⬇️

latestPosts.caption:
💡If you’ve been skipping over butternut squash at the market, you’re missing out on a nutritional powerhouse that supports metabolic, cardiovascular, immune, digestive, and even cognitive health—all in one delicious, low-GI package. 🍽✨

🤓As a Board Certified Functional Medicine Practitioner, degreed and experienced in nursing and mental health & addictions counseling (including food addiction), and also a Certified Lifestyle Weight Management Specialist, Certified Specialist in Menopause Coaching, and Certified GLP-1 Exercise Specialist, I examine food not just for macronutrients—but for how it supports the entire system: metabolically, hormonally, neurologically, and emotionally.

🌟Butternut squash deserves a permanent spot on your plate—here’s why 👇

🔶 Rich in carotenes (Pro-Vitamin A) for immunity, vision, and inflammation support 🧬🧡

🔶 Vitamin C supports collagen, adrenal health, and mood 💥😃

🔶 Potassium for blood pressure, nerve + muscle health ⚡️🫀

🔶 Full of B1, B3, B5, B6, Folate—vital for energy, hormones & brain 🌿🧠

🔶 ~7g of fiber per cup—great for digestion, satiety & hormone clearance 💩🌾

🔶 Low glycemic index—supports blood sugar balance & cravings 📉🍠

🔶 Winter squash = long shelf life when harvested & stored properly. Keeps for months! 🧺🌞

🍲Roast it, mash it, spiralize it, or blend it into soup. It’s comfort food with benefits. 🍜🔥

How do you like your butternut squash? 👇

latestPosts.caption:
Feeling like staying healthy is impossible when life gets chaotic? You're not alone — but there's hope and strategy 💡.

🤓 As a Board Certified Functional Medicine Practitioner, degreed and experienced in nursing and mental health and addictions counseling (including food addictions), and also a Certified Lifestyle Weight Management Specialist, Certified Specialist in Menopause Coaching, and Certified GLP-1 Exercise Specialist — I understand how overwhelming it can be to stay consistent when life doesn’t slow down.

Here’s the truth: You don’t need to overhaul your life. You just need a system that works for your reality.

Today’s carousel shares real-life wellness habits that stick:

👉 1. Frozen fruits & veggies = budget-friendly + no prep

👉 2. Make healthy food visible 🍓🥬 = eat more of it

👉 3. Hide/separate junk food = less temptation

👉 4. Meal prep weekly ✅ Glass containers > plastic

👉 5. Never leave home without healthy food = no hangry decisions 🚫

👉 6. Clean proteins delivered 🐟🥩-- DM us for our go-to vendor and a discount!

👉 7. Consider a healthy meal box = time-saver!

💡 Consistency comes from preparation, not motivation. 

If you're on a GLP-1 or navigating perimenopause/menopause, these habits are key for blood sugar, metabolism, & cravings.

✨ Your health doesn’t have to be complicated.

📌 Save this for when life gets messy (because it will).

latestPosts.caption:
🤓As a Board Certified Functional Medicine Practitioner, degreed in nursing and mental health & addictions counseling, a Certified Lifestyle Weight Management Specialist, Certified Specialist in Menopause Coaching, and a Certified GLP-1 Exercise Specialist, I look at food through the lens of whole-body systems — hormones, metabolism, mental health, inflammation, and beyond.

Cauliflower checks far more boxes than most people realize. Let’s break it down ⬇️

🥦 1. Vitamin K
Supports calcium regulation, bone health, cognition & inflammation reduction.

🍊 2. Vitamin C
Over half your daily needs per serving—great for immunity, collagen, & antioxidant defense.

🌾 3. Fiber
Supports gut health, blood sugar, and satiety—ideal for weight & GLP-1 support.

💪 4. Potassium + Phosphorus
Crucial for hydration, nerve function, bone strength—especially in menopause & fat loss.

🧠 5. B Vitamins (esp. Folate)
Key for mental health, energy, neurotransmitter balance & stress resilience.

🧬 6. Cancer-Fighting Compounds
Glucosinolates + isothiocyanates block harmful enzymes & boost detoxification.

⚠️ 7. Goitrogens + Thyroid
Raw cruciferous veggies in large amounts can impair thyroid in iodine-deficient or thyroid-compromised individuals—but cooking reduces this.

🍽 8. Low GI + High Satiety
Won’t spike blood sugar. Supports satiety & metabolic balance.

💡The Takeaway:
More than trendy, cauliflower is a low-calorie, nutrient-dense, hormone-supporting, gut-loving superfood.

📌 Save this for your next meal plan.

🥦 Drop a 🧠 if you're eating smart on purpose.

👩‍⚕️ Follow for science-backed nutrition insights.

latestPosts.caption:
🌟🧠Anxiety isn’t just in your head — it’s in your biochemistry, too.

🤓As a Board Certified Functional Medicine Practitioner, with degrees and work experience in nursing and mental health & addictions counseling, a certified specialist in lifestyle weight management, menopause coaching, and GLP-1 exercise, and more, I’ve seen firsthand how the right nutraceuticals can support the brain and nervous system in profound ways.

📋Here are 8 nutrients science shows may help manage anxiety — especially when combined with good sleep, movement, and blood sugar balance (see our past carousels):

🔬 1. L-Theanine
Naturally calming. Found in tea. Boosts alpha brain waves, easing mental stress without sedation.

🔬 2. Vitamin D (w/ Magnesium + K2)
A mood stabilizer. Needs magnesium & K2 to function properly. 

🔬 3. B-Complex Vitamins
Support energy, brain function, stress response. Best in a quality multi with minerals—these work as a team.

🔬 4. Omega-3s
EPA & DHA support emotional regulation and reduce brain inflammation.

🔬 5. 5-HTP
A serotonin precursor. May reduce anxious thoughts, especially when dietary tryptophan is low.

🔬 6. Magnesium (Threonate)
Crosses the blood-brain barrier. Calms the mind at the source.

🔬 7. GABA
Your brain’s “off switch.” Promotes calm, reduces reactivity, supports better sleep.

🔬 8. Phosphatidylserine
Supports brain structure and lowers cortisol — key for daily stress resilience.

👉 Always consult your personal healthcare provider before starting supplements. This is education, not medical advice. 

Food first, but strategic support can help too. 

Click our link in bio, and scroll to where it says Fullscript to save 20% on professional-grade and authentic supplements.

latestPosts.caption:
🌟Overlooking blackberries? You’re missing one of nature’s most powerful, nutrient-dense allies for whole-body health.

🤓As a Board Certified Functional Medicine Practitioner—with degrees in nursing and in mental health & addictions counseling, plus specialist certifications in Lifestyle Weight Management, Menopause Coaching, and GLP-1 Exercise—I take a food-as-medicine approach seriously. 🫐💪

Here’s why blackberries are a top performer:

👉 Antioxidants: Rich in anthocyanins, blackberries fight oxidative stress, support brain health, aging, and inflammation regulation.

👉 Vitamin C: One cup = ~50% daily needs. Crucial for immunity, collagen production, iron absorption, and stress resilience—especially during menopause.

👉 Fiber: With 8g per cup, blackberries aid gut health, balance blood sugar, and support satiety. Fiber also fuels gut-brain connection and mood regulation.

👉 Manganese: Vital for bone formation, enzyme activity, metabolism, and connective tissue health.

👉 Anthocyanins: These flavonoids promote heart and brain health and may reduce neuroinflammation—important for cognitive clarity and mood.

👉 Vitamin K: Supports bone strength, blood clotting, and hormonal balance in life phases like menopause.

👉 Low Glycemic & Satiating: Low-GI + high-fiber = stable blood sugar, appetite control, and metabolic support. Great for GLP-1-informed eating.

🧬 From gut to brain, hormone to metabolism—blackberries deliver. Low in calories, rich in micronutrients, and perfect for any wellness plan.

🍇 Pro tip: Toss in smoothies, on Greek yogurt, or freeze for a refreshing snack.

🧐 How do you enjoy blackberries? Share below!

latestPosts.caption:
You already know exercise helps you “stay in shape” — but as a Board Certified Functional Medicine Practitioner and more, I can tell you: the benefits go far beyond the mirror. 🧬💭

If you think of exercise only as weight loss or muscle gain, you’re missing out on some of its most powerful, life-enhancing effects!

Here are 8 you may not know 👇

1️⃣ It helps grow brain cells.
Exercise boosts BDNF, which supports neurogenesis — your brain’s ability to grow new neurons. That means better memory, focus, and learning. 🧠✨

2️⃣ It builds mitochondria.
Strength training or cardio triggers mitochondrial biogenesis. More mitochondria = more energy, better metabolism, healthier aging. 🔋

3️⃣ It reduces stress.
Exercise lowers cortisol and boosts endorphins + endocannabinoids. With my background in mental health + addictions counseling, I’ve seen how real this is. 🌿

4️⃣ It strengthens immunity.
Activity helps circulate natural killer and T-cells — your body’s defense team. 🛡

5️⃣ It improves sleep and energy.
Movement helps regulate your circadian rhythm — so you fall asleep easier and wake more refreshed. 🛌☀️

6️⃣ It supports longevity.
Exercise reduces inflammation, improves metabolic and heart health, and lowers chronic disease risk. 🫀

7️⃣ It protects muscle during weight loss.
As a Certified Weight Management + GLP-1 Exercise Specialist, I stress resistance training to help preserve skeletal muscle during any manner of weight loss. 💪🏽⚖️

8️⃣ It helps with menopause transitions.
Targeted movement supports body comp and energy. As a Menopause Coaching Specialist, I see this benefit often overlooked. 🌙🔥

👉 Exercise isn’t just about what you lose — it’s about what you gain!

latestPosts.caption:
🍓 STRAWBERRIES: NUTRITION THAT WORKS FOR YOU 🍓

As a Board Certified Functional Medicine Practitioner and Certified Specialist in Menopause, GLP-1 Exercise, and Lifestyle Weight Management, I highlight foods with clinical value across systems — and strawberries are one of them.

👩‍⚕️ 1. Natural Metabolic Support
Low in calories + high in fiber = blood sugar balance + appetite control. Their low glycemic index makes them ideal for insulin resistance, prediabetes, or GLP-1 protocols.

💪 2. Vitamin C Power
1 cup = 100%+ of daily needs. Supports immune function, collagen, adrenal health, and fat metabolism — key in perimenopause/menopause when collagen declines + cortisol rises.

🧠 3. Anthocyanins for Brain + Heart
The red color comes from anthocyanins, compounds shown to reduce oxidative stress + support vascular and cognitive function — essential for aging bodies.

🧬 4. Ellagic Acid: Anti-Inflammatory + Protective
This polyphenol has anti-inflammatory + anti-cancer properties, helping fight inflammation at the cellular level.

🌱 5. Fiber for Fullness + Gut Health
3g fiber per cup = better satiety, regularity, and microbiome diversity. Low-GI, high-fiber fruits help curb cravings without metabolic chaos.

🧂 6. Potassium: The Overlooked Electrolyte
Crucial for blood pressure, hydration, and muscle function — especially in exercise, GLP-1 training, and menopause transitions.

💡 Functional Medicine Takeaway:
Strawberries are more than a topping. They support hormone balance, metabolic flexibility, cognitive health, and gut function. Choose organic when possible — they top the EWG Dirty Dozen list.

latestPosts.caption:
You're exhausted. Gaining weight. Foggy. Cold all the time. Losing hair. Labs say you're “normal”…
But how you feel is anything but normal.

As a Board Certified Functional Medicine Practitioner, Certified Lifestyle Weight Management Specialist, Certified Menopause Coaching Specialist, and more, I help clients figure out why they feel “off” — even when they've been told "everything looks fine."

Often the real issue?
👉 Suboptimal thyroid function.

🧠🔥 Your thyroid drives the metabolism of every cell in your body!

This isn’t just about fatigue or weight — it’s about cellular energy, gene expression, tissue repair, and hormone signaling.

When thyroid hormones (especially T3) are low or poorly converted, it affects:

🔋 Mitochondrial energy (ATP)
🧬 Cellular repair
🧠 Neurotransmitters
💥 Insulin + GLP-1 response
🩸 Detox pathways
🔥 Your entire metabolic engine

But most conventional doctors only check TSH, which is a brain hormone — not a thyroid hormone — and only flags after dysfunction has progressed.

Worse, many conventional providers still use TSH ranges that were known to be inadequate even in the 1990s (this exact issue led me to nursing school)!

🔍 Functional medicine looks deeper!  See carousel.

Why? Because your thyroid doesn’t act alone — it’s in constant conversation with your adrenals, gut, liver, and sex hormones.

Conventional providers rarely check if:

⚠️ T4 is converting to T3
⚠️ Reverse T3 is blocking receptors
⚠️ Stress + midlife hormone shifts are disrupting signaling

And YOU feel the symptoms — even if the labs & provider say “you’re fine.”

If you’ve been dismissed, misdiagnosed, or no longer feel like yourself…

🔁 Save + share if this resonates.

latestPosts.caption:
💡🧠Anxiety isn’t just in your head—it’s in your habits, hormones, sleep, and nervous system.

🔦Before supplements, I focus on what’s already within your control.

🤓As a Board Certified Functional Medicine Practitioner, degreed in mental health and addictions counseling, Certified Lifestyle Weight Management Specialist, Menopause Coaching Specialist, and GLP-1 Exercise Specialist, I guide clients through simple, science-backed shifts that support long-term anxiety relief.

🌿🌟This carousel highlights foundational strategies I recommend for anxiety—whether it’s chronic, situational, or hormonally driven.

👉 (Hormonally driven anxiety at midlife often improves with bio-identical hormone restoration.)

👉 Move with intention.
Support nervous system health with regular, blood sugar-stabilizing movement—not burnout workouts.

👉 Prioritize sleep.
You can’t out-supplement poor sleep. Rest is where the healing happens.

👉 Eat for a calm brain.
Reduce omega-6s. Prioritize magnesium, omega-3s, and blood sugar balance. Food is chemistry.

👉 Activate your parasympathetic nervous system.
Use breathwork, cold exposure, or legitimate vagus nerve stimulators to shift from fight-or-flight to rest-and-repair.

👉 Ditch alcohol and nicotine.
They may numb anxiety short-term but worsen it over time.

👉 Connect.
Bonding with people or pets boosts oxytocin, calming the fear center of the brain.

These aren't trends—they’re essentials.

✨ Save this to revisit.
✨ Share it with someone ready to shift.
✨ Follow for more anxiety support grounded in functional medicine and psychology.

latestPosts.caption:
If you have PCOS and weight loss feels impossible, this is probably why.
Up to 70% of women with PCOS have insulin resistance—meaning your cells don’t respond well to insulin. Your body compensates by making MORE insulin, which:
Triggers more testosterone production (unwanted hair and acne)
Makes your body STORE fat instead of burning it Creates intense cravings for sugar and carbs Makes you feel exhausted even after sleeping
This is why traditional “eat less, move more” advice doesn’t work for PCOS. Your hormones are working against you.
When you eat in a way that manages insulin— balancing protein, fiber, and healthy fats—your body can finally start responding the way it should.

Want to know exactly what to eat? I created a free
7-day PCOS meal plan with balanced meals designed to support insulin sensitivity- link in bio

For educational purposes only. Discuss weight concerns with qualified healthcare providers.

latestPosts.caption:
You can eat perfectly and exercise daily… but if your stress hormones are elevated, your body is fighting against you.
Cortisol isn’t the enemy—but chronic stress is. 

Save and follow for more PCOS and hormone tips

#WeightLoss #CortisolAndWeightGain #StressAndHormones #PCOS #HormoneHealth

Stress isn’t just “in your head” — it’s in your hormones.
If you have PCOS, high cortisol can worsen insulin resistance, increase androgens, and make symptoms harder to manage. 

Healing PCOS starts with managing stress

Your hormones are listening to every signal you send. Small, consistent changes make a big difference.

The good news? You have more control than you think.

Save this for your next stressful day!

#PCOS #PCOSAwareness #CortisolBalance #HormoneHealth #PCOSSupport

Travel doesn’t have to wreck your hormones Skipped meals, poor sleep, and constant stress can throw off cortisol and blood sugar — especially if you have PCOS or hormone imbalance.
These 3 travel hacks help your body feel supported, not stressed:
Morning sunlight = circadian reset
Protein snacks = blood sugar stability
Movement breaks = cortisol control

Traveling soon? Don’t let it tank your hormones
Save this for your next trip!

#HormoneHealth #PCOSSupport #WomensWellness #HormoneBalance
#HealthyTravel

You don’t have to choose between enjoying Christmas dinner and balanced hormones

The Hormone-Friendly CHRISTMAS PLATE: protein first (turkey, ham), then healthy fats (butter, gravy, nuts), then complex carbs

STEP 1: Protein first:

WHY IT WORKS:
Protein signals satiety hormones 
(leptin, GLP-1) and prevents the 
blood sugar spike that comes 
when you start with carbs.

STEP 2: Healthy Fats

WHY IT WORKS:
Fat slows glucose absorption 
into bloodstream AND helps you 
absorb vitamins A, D, E, K from 
your vegetables.

STEP 3: Complex Carbs

WHY IT WORKS:
When eaten AFTER protein + fat, 
these carbs won’t spike your 
blood sugar as dramatically.

STEP 4: Dessert (YES!) 
Make it hormone-friendly:

WHY IT WORKS:
The protein and fat you’ve 
already eaten buffer the 
glucose response.

Want more hormone-friendly 
strategies that work in REAL life?

latestPosts.caption:
Your morning routine that might be messing with your hormones
Skipping breakfast can spike cortisol and worsen blood sugar swings — especially with PCOS.
Checking your phone before natural light disrupts cortisol rhythm and sleep hormones.
Coffee on an empty stomach can worsen anxiety, PMS, and hormone imbalance.
Doing high-intensity workouts first thing — especially fasted — can increase cortisol and worsen hormone symptoms.

Morning habits that may be throwing off your hormones
If you’re dealing with PCOS, PMS, irregular cycles, fatigue, or anxiety — how you start your morning matters more than you think.
Your cortisol is naturally highest in the morning.
Stacking intense workouts, fasting, coffee on an empty stomach, and stress can amplify hormone symptoms — especially for hormonally sensitive women.
- The fix isn’t doing more.
It’s supporting your body’s rhythm:
• Eat within 60 minutes
• Get natural light first
• Choose gentle movement when needed
• Fuel before intense workouts
Small shifts = better hormone balance.

#PCOSSupport
#HormoneBalance
#WomensWellness

 Once you know what’s happening, you can fix it. Lifestyle interventions for PCOS are incredibly powerful.

latestPosts.caption:
4 PCOS types. Different treatments.
Which one are you?
FREE quiz in bio —> Click on Linktree to find the link on the top of the page! 

Disclaimer: For educational purposes only, not medical advice
#PCOS #PCOSTypes #PCOSQuiz

Important to know:
• You do not need ovarian cysts to have PCOS
• Blood work and symptoms matter more than ultrasound alone
• Other conditions (thyroid, prolactin, adrenal disorders) should be ruled out
Bottom line:
PCOS is a clinical diagnosis, based on patterns — not a single scan or lab result.

#PCOSDiagnosis #PCOS #PCOSAwareness #RotterdamCriteria #Hyperandrogenism #WomensHealth #HormoneHealth

1. Spearmint Tea — Androgen & PCOS Support
• Helps reduce excess androgens
• Can improve acne and unwanted hair growth
• Supports more regular cycles
Great daily tea for women with PCOS or irregular periods

2. Raspberry Leaf Tea — Cycle Strength & Period Support
• Tones uterine muscles
• Eases menstrual cramping
• Supports smoother cycles
Ideal for luteal phase or the week before your period

3. Ginger Tea — Inflammation & Estrogen Support
• Helps lower inflammation (a major hormone disruptor)
• Supports healthy digestion and blood sugar
• May help reduce estrogen dominance symptoms
Perfect first-thing-in-the-morning tea

4. Chamomile Tea — Cortisol + Sleep Regulation
• Naturally calms the nervous system
• Helps reduce cortisol spikes
• Supports deeper, more restorative sleep
Amazing for stress, PMS, and perimenopause-related insomnia

Small daily habits create BIG hormonal shifts.
If you want simple, evidence-based hormone support, save this post and start with one tea a day. 

#hormonebalance
#hormonehealth
#womenshormones
#herbaltea
#herbalremedies

Most women have a combination of types 1 & 2 - which is why addressing BOTH insulin AND inflammation is so important.

There's always two sides to this 👀🤦🏻‍♀️

The ones who always post their fitness and the ones who wouldn't even think about posting. And honestly, both are absolutely okay.

For my posts personally, I've heard the comments of "here she goes again posting her fitness life on socials" 👀😳
And when I haven't posted in a while, I've had direct messages saying "are you okay?? Your posts really motivate me to keep going and I haven't seen any" 

If like me, posting keeps you committed to your journey, please, please don't stop sharing. Yes not everyone may like it, but you never know who you may be inspiring to keep going.
It's a difficult journey, it's not easy by any means. 

Let's keep each other motivated, eating healthy and moving in 2026 🙌🏻💖

latestPosts.caption:
Stress doesn’t just affect your mood. It affects your brain.

For people living with seizures, stress is a quiet but powerful trigger.
When stress hormones rise, the brain becomes overstimulated.

Add poor sleep, emotional pressure, or physical exhaustion, and the risk of a seizure increases.

That’s why seizure care isn’t only about medication.
It’s also about:
 • good sleep
• calm routines
• stress control
• and never skipping prescribed drugs

If seizures are becoming more frequent, or stress feels out of control, it’s important to talk to a specialist early.

📍Our Neurology Unit is available at 4 Raji Rasaki Estate Road, Amuwo Odofin, Lagos, for proper evaluation and long-term care.

#PanoxMultispecialistHospital
#BrainHealth
#NeurologyCare
#SeizureAwareness 
#StressAndHealth

latestPosts.caption:
It happens.
Festive meals, celebrations, late nights… sometimes we eat past comfort before our body can say “I’m full.”

The good news?
Your body is incredibly intelligent and forgiving when you support it properly.

Instead of punishing yourself or skipping meals, focus on recovery, not restriction.

Overeating once won’t harm you, how you respond afterward matters more.

Listen to your body. Support it. Let it do what it does best: heal and rebalance.

If discomfort lingers, pain persists, or bloating becomes frequent, that’s your cue to seek professional care. 

At Panox, we don’t just treat symptoms, we help you understand your body.

📍 Visit the Gastroenterology Unit at Panox Multi specialist Hospital at 4 Raji Rasaki Estate Road, Amuwo Odofin, Lagos for expert digestive care.

#DigestiveHealth #GutCare #HealthyHabits
#PanoxHospital #listentoyourbody

Holiday travel is exciting, but it can quietly strain your body.

Long hours on the road, crowded terminals, skipped meals, poor hydration… it all adds up.

Staying safe this season isn’t about fear.
It’s about preparedness.

Simple habits like resting when tired, washing your hands, staying hydrated, and keeping your medications close can prevent avoidable health emergencies, especially for children, older adults, and anyone managing a chronic condition.

At Panox, we want you to arrive at your destination with joy, not symptoms.
Travel well. Travel wisely. Your health should arrive with you. 🤍

Orange Juice & Blood Pressure: What You Should Know 🍊❤️
Can drinking orange juice every day help your blood pressure? Research suggests it might offer modest heart benefits — but the details matter.

🍊 What the Studies Found
Some studies show that orange juice may help improve blood vessel function and support healthy blood pressure thanks to its vitamin C, potassium, and antioxidant content. But the effects are generally small — not a replacement for proven treatments or lifestyle changes.

⚠️ What to Watch Out For
• Orange juice contains natural sugars, which can raise blood sugar if you drink too much
• Whole oranges offer more fiber and are often a better choice
• People managing diabetes or watching calories should be mindful of portion sizes
💡 How to Make It Heart-Smart
• Stick to 4–6 oz (a small glass)
• Choose 100% orange juice with no added sugar
• Pair it with protein or fiber to reduce sugar spikes

❤️ Bottom Line
Orange juice can be part of a heart-healthy diet — but it’s not a stand-alone fix for high blood pressure. Enjoy it in moderation and continue focusing on balanced meals, movement, stress management, and your prescribed treatments.

Read more - https://www.verywellhealth.com/blood-pressure-orange-juice-everyday-11831868 

#orangejuice #orange #hypertension #bloodpressure #bloodpressureawareness #highbloodpresure #BloodPressureSupport #hearthealth #heartdisease

♨️ Hot Tubs & Heart Health: What You Should Know Before You Soak
Hot tubs feel relaxing — but they can pose hidden risks, especially if you have a heart condition or high blood pressure.

⚠️ Why Hot Tubs Can Be Risky
When you soak in hot water:
Your heart rate speeds up, and blood vessels dilate.
Blood pressure can drop suddenly, straining your cardiovascular system.
These rapid changes — temperature + circulation — can trigger dizziness, fainting, or worse, even heart attack in people with underlying conditions.

✅ Who Should Be Cautious
Anyone with high blood pressure, heart disease, or circulation issues
People using medications that affect blood flow or blood pressure
Seniors or those with autonomic/nerve sensitivity

🔎 Smart Tips If You Still Want to Soak
Keep the water temperature moderate — avoid overly hot baths.
Limit soak time to 10–15 minutes max.
Avoid alcohol or medications that relax blood vessels before bathing.
Always enter and exit water slowly.
Stay alert for warning signs: dizziness, chest discomfort, abnormal heart rhythm.

Read more - https://health.clevelandclinic.org/hot-tubs-can-land-your-heart-in-hot-water

#hypertension #highbloodpressure #hearthealth #bloodpressure #bloodpressuresupport

🎄❤️ Holiday Foods to Limit for Healthier Blood Pressure
The holidays bring amazing food — but some festive favorites can quietly raise your blood pressure. Being mindful of a few common ingredients can help you enjoy the season without the unwanted BP spike.
🧂 Processed Meats
Holiday hams, sausages, and bacon are often loaded with sodium and saturated fats — a double hit for blood pressure. Enjoy in small portions or choose leaner, unprocessed cuts.
🍰 Saturated Fats
Cakes, cookies, pastries, pies, butter, ghee, coconut oil, full-fat dairy — all can contribute to higher BP and added cardiovascular strain. Opt for lighter desserts or lower-fat options when possible.
🍖 Red Meat
Beef, pork, lamb, venison, and other red meats may raise blood pressure, and the compounds released during digestion can increase it even further. Stick to smaller portions or choose leaner cuts.
🥨 Salty Snacks
Chips, crackers, holiday mixes — easy to overeat and packed with hidden sodium. Swap for nuts, veggies, or lower-salt options.
🍹 Holiday Cocktails
Alcohol raises BP when overdone — and festive drinks with salt rims or sugary mixers add extra strain. Sip mindfully and stay hydrated.
✨ Small choices make a big difference.

A little awareness at the holiday table helps protect your heart while still enjoying the season.

Read more - https://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/foods-to-avoid-with-high-blood-pressure#red-meat

#hypertension #bloodpressure #hearthealth #healthylife

❄️ Stay Heart-Healthy This Holiday Season ❤️
The holidays are full of joy — but they can also challenge your blood pressure if you aren’t mindful. Experts say it’s easy to let routines slip, but keeping your BP steady now helps you enjoy the season and protect your heart.

🍽️ Think Before You Feast
Holiday foods can be tempting, but enjoying consciously makes a difference. Eat a healthy meal beforehand and look for vegetables, lean proteins, and lower-sodium options at gatherings.

🧂 Watch the Sodium
Excess sodium pulls water into your blood vessels and raises BP. Processed, canned, preserved foods — and even festive hams — can be especially high in salt. Cook with less salt and let guests season at the table.

🍷 Mind the “Cup of Cheer”
Alcohol can raise blood pressure, especially around the holidays. Drink in moderation and skip salt-rimmed cocktails like margaritas.

🧘 Stick to a Healthy Routine
Eat breakfast, get daily movement, manage stress, and don’t skip medications. Simple consistency helps steady blood pressure even during busy holiday weeks.

💛 Bottom Line
A little awareness — from what you eat to how you manage stress — helps you enjoy the season while keeping your heart protected. Celebrate smart, stay steady, and take care of yourself this holiday.

Read more - https://www.heart.org/en/news/2022/12/06/keeping-high-blood-pressure-at-bay-for-the-holidays

#Hypertension #bloodpressure #highbloodpressure #BloodPressureManagement #winter #wintervibes #coldweather

Apple Cider Vinegar & Blood Pressure: What’s the Real Story? 🍎❤️
Apple cider vinegar (ACV) is trending everywhere — but can it actually help with high blood pressure?

Here’s what we know from current research:
🔍 ACV is not a proven blood-pressure treatment
There’s no strong scientific evidence that ACV lowers hypertension. It may support overall health, but it should never replace prescribed medication or monitoring.

🌿 Potential Indirect Benefits
ACV may support:
• Better blood sugar control
• Modest weight management
• Improved digestion
Since healthy weight and stable blood sugar support heart health, ACV can play a small, indirect role in your wellness routine — but it’s not a primary BP solution.

⚠️ Use It Safely
• Always dilute ACV — it’s acidic and can harm teeth or your stomach
• Avoid taking it in large amounts
• Be cautious if you take medications like diuretics, insulin, or potassium-sparing drugs

💙 Bottom Line
ACV can be part of a healthy lifestyle, but it’s not a substitute for medical treatment or blood-pressure management. Stick with evidence-based care — and talk to your provider before adding ACV regularly to your routine.

Read more - https://www.healthline.com/health/high-blood-pressure-hypertension/acv 

#bloodpressure #Hypertension #BloodPressureManagement #highbloodpressure  #applecidervinegar #ACV

Heart-Healthy Stocking Stuffers for Loved Ones with Hypertension ❤️🎁
Holiday gifting doesn’t always have to be big — sometimes the small, thoughtful things make the biggest difference.
If you’re filling stockings this season, here are simple category-based ideas that support heart health and help manage high blood pressure:
🌿 Heart-Healthy Snack Ideas
Think low-sodium nuts, dark chocolate, or herbal teas that feel festive and supportive.
💧 Hydration Boosters
Cute water bottles or fruit-infusion ideas encourage daily hydration — a key part of BP control.
🧂 Flavor Without the Salt
Seasonings, herbs, garlic, or citrus-based flavor options help reduce sodium without sacrificing taste.
🧘 Stress-Relief Mini Gifts
Candles, journals, aromatherapy rollers, or stress balls help calm the mind — because stress affects BP too.
🚶 Move More Essentials
Resistance bands, stretch bands, or step-goal reminders keep physical activity fun and accessible.
📒 Routine & Tracking Helpers
Pill organizers, wellness planners, or gentle medication reminders can support consistent heart-healthy habits.
✨ Small gifts, big heart impact.

Perfect for friends, family, or anyone living with hypertension.
Healthy living can be thoughtful — and festive too. ❤️🎄

❄️ Winter & Your Heart: Why Cold Weather Raises Blood Pressure and Risk
As temperatures drop, your heart has to work harder — and that can mean higher blood pressure and increased risk of heart attack or stroke, especially for people with hypertension.
🔎 Why Winter Affects Your Heart
Blood vessels constrict in the cold, which raises resistance and makes your heart pump harder.
Blood pressure naturally increases during colder months, even in people without hypertension.
Cold + exertion is a risky combo — activities like shoveling snow or sudden heavy physical effort put extra strain on your heart.
People with existing heart disease or high BP may face higher chances of angina, heart attack, or stroke during winter.
✅ How to Protect Yourself This Season
Dress warmly and avoid sudden exposure to cold air.
Check your blood pressure more frequently during cold spells.
Stay active — but choose indoor or gentle exercise instead of heavy outdoor exertion.
Keep up heart-healthy habits: balanced diet, hydration, and regular medications.
Know your symptoms and act fast if something feels off.

✨ Bottom Line
Winter changes how your blood vessels and heart behave — but with awareness and smart habits, you can stay safe. Stay warm, stay steady, and look after your heart this season. ❤️❄️

Read more - https://www.healthline.com/health-news/why-hypertension-heart-attack-stroke-risks-are-higher-in-the-winter

#winter #bloodpressure #highbloodpressure #Hypertension #health #hearthealth #heart #bloodpressuremanagement

Red Wine & Blood Pressure: What You Should Know 🍷⚠️
Love a glass of red wine now and then? 🍇 While red wine is often hailed as “heart-healthy,” the science says — don’t count on it to lower your blood pressure. Here’s the real story:
Though red wine contains antioxidants like resveratrol, the amounts needed for actual blood-pressure benefits are much higher than what you’d normally drink. 

In fact, regular or heavy drinking of red wine often raises blood pressure, especially over time. 

Some small studies suggest non-alcoholic red wine might offer modest benefits — but that doesn’t apply to regular wine with alcohol. 

If you have high blood pressure or take BP medications, even moderate alcohol intake can interfere with your treatment or increase risk. 

✅ Bottom line: Red wine isn’t a reliable way to manage hypertension. If you drink, do so sparingly — and treat lifestyle, diet, exercise, and meds as your top tools for heart health.
Stay wise, stay heart-aware. ❤️

Read more - https://www.verywellhealth.com/does-red-wine-lower-blood-pressure-11776501

#redwine #hypertension #hypertensionawareness #highbloodpressure #bloodpressure #hearthealth #heart #health #wine

#Traveling This Holiday Season? Keep Your Heart in Mind. ❤️✈️
Holiday trips are exciting, but they can also bring extra challenges if you’re managing heart disease, high blood pressure, or stroke risk. A little preparation goes a long way toward a safer, more enjoyable journey.

🩺 Check in with your healthcare provider
Discuss your travel plans, get medical guidance if needed, and keep important health documents and emergency contacts with you.

💊 Pack and organize medications
Bring enough medication for the entire trip (plus extra), keep them properly labeled, and consider time-zone shifts if your dosing schedule matters. Don’t forget devices like CPAPs, glucose monitors, or portable oxygen if you use them.

📍 Know your destination
Research climate, altitude, and the nearest medical facilities. Extreme weather or high elevations can impact heart conditions, so knowing what to expect is key.

🚶 Move often & stay hydrated
Long flights or road trips can raise the risk of swelling or blood clots. Stand, stretch, walk when you can, wear compression socks if advised, and drink water regularly.

😌 Manage stress
Holiday travel can be hectic. Give yourself extra time, plan ahead, and take breaks to keep your stress — and blood pressure — in a healthier range.

✨ Bottom line:
With a little preparation, you can enjoy your holiday travels while protecting your heart health every step of the way.

Read more - https://newsroom.heart.org/news/make-a-list-check-it-twice-with-these-heart-healthy-holiday-travel-hacks

#hypertension #bloodpressure #highbloodpressure #hearthealth #travel #traveltips #stressfreetravel

💙 Simple Foods That Help Lower Blood Pressure
Supporting your heart doesn’t always require major changes — sometimes, it starts right on your plate. 

Here are three smart foods that can help keep your blood pressure in check:

🥬 Leafy Greens
Spinach, kale, arugula, and other greens are rich in natural nitrates that help relax blood vessels and improve circulation.

🍌 Bananas
High in potassium, bananas help your body balance sodium levels and ease pressure on your arterial walls.

🐟 Salmon
Full of omega-3 fatty acids, salmon helps reduce inflammation and supports overall cardiovascular health.

✨ Small choices add up.
Incorporate these foods regularly for steady, long-term heart support. Your heart will thank you! ❤️

Read more - https://www.healthline.com/health/high-blood-pressure/guide-foods-for-hypertension-high-blood-pressure#foods-to-eat

latestPosts.caption:
Rugby truth bomb: your bro split isn’t saving you in a lineout.
And no, 5-day chest–arms–pump sessions don’t count as “explosive power training.” 😂💥

Been working with a back row/front row utility forward recently, and the transformation has been chef’s kiss.
We’ve dialled in:
⚡ Explosive power
⚡ Overcoming inertia
⚡ Rate of force development
⚡ Actual athletic strength — not just mirror muscles

The result?
He left No.5 Lock for dead at the front of a lineout.
Beat him to the movement.
Beat him in the vertical explosion.
Beat him everywhere.
That’s what an athletic strength programme does. 🤷‍♂️💪

If you want to move like an athlete — not just look like one — it might be time to rethink the old-school gym routine…

👉 Hitchin players: ready to upgrade from “bro strong” to rugby strong?

This dedicated Herts/Cambridge cricketer has just levelled up to the Poliquin Step/Squat 👏🏏
But before we jump into cricket-specific power work, we always build the foundations first. 🧱

He came in with a history of those frustrating little niggles… so instead of guessing, we went all in on understanding the root cause.
🔍 Full movement investigation
🧠 Motor-control assessment
⚙️ Biomechanics review

And what showed up?
Very clear, sub-optimal patterns that were limiting his performance and efficiency. 🚧

So we got to work.
Using targeted techniques to rebuild:
🤝 Balance
🎯 Control
🛡️ Stability
— the platform every athlete needs before adding power, speed, and skill.

This is how you unlock real, long-term performance. 💥🏏
Stronger foundations = stronger athlete.

Yep. We’re talking full-on G-forces like a mini roller coaster… strapped to your legs. 🎢💥

Nope. Not gonna cut the mustard. 🛑🥫

A proper strength plan needs a few weeks to build:
🧱 strength,

💪 hypertrophy — all the good stuff that keeps your knees from screaming halfway down the slope. 🔊🦵

So if you want:
✔️ fewer injuries,
✔️ faster turns, and
✔️ a season that doesn’t end in the lodge with hot chocolate and regret… ☕😩

Start your Ski Prehab NOW.
Because Prehab > Rehab every single time.

DM me for ski prep programs. 🎿

latestPosts.caption:
💪 ADAPT. EVOLVE. OVERCOME. ⚽️🔥

S&C isn’t just about lifting — it’s about adaptation and problem solving.

Meet Izzy, a talented Junior Premiership League footballer with Luton FC ⚽️. Like many young athletes pushing their limits, she’s tackling Osgood Schlatter’s, a painful growth-related condition affecting the tendon just below the kneecap.

But setbacks don’t stop her 👊 — they shape her.

We’re adapting her resistance training to support her rehab, maintain strength, and prevent muscle loss while her body heals and develops.

Her dedication and work ethic are elite — it’s a privilege to work with an athlete this focused and resilient 💥

👉 Keep an eye on this one — future star in the making 🌟

latestPosts.caption:
Full disclosure. Haven’t lifted anything ‘heavy’ for a long time. Certainly well over a year (Pre-arthritic from contact sports, a previous - slightly hazardous job, aging and surgeries) 

I tend to lift KBs in 10-12 ranges these days rather than 3-5s.

But had an absolute 🐻 of an athlete over this week. Quite a bit younger than me 🦖 The old competitive devil 😈 on the shoulder said ‘why don’t you have a little try?!) 

So a daft 50 year old in Hitchin ‘had a little go’. (I still like to know I can have a ‘wrestle’ 🤼 when required)

Results weren’t bad for an older fella who hasn’t lifted properly for yonks. 😊 Respectable. But need to stay less intense for the knees… I need them to walk for a few years yet!

This is for all my Mentees who doing program with me.
You’re heading out with friends and the anxiety is creeping up. You want to stick to the weight loss program but you’ve been looking forward to this meal out for ages! 

Never fear - I  can help you. Here are some tips for staying in control when out:

😎look at the menu before you go. Most places have their menus online. Have a look and make a plan. If you’re not sure, reach out and your mentor can help.

😎everyone else sharing? Don’t be ashamed to order your own meal and be honest. This won’t be forever and if they’re good friends, they’ll understand.

😎Soda and lime instead of a bottle of wine. 

😎put some balance drops under your tongue just before you go out. This will help balance your sugars and not crave the bread so much. 

😎nothing on the menu? Ask for grilled fish, paneer or chicken with steamed green veg. You’d be surprised how many places do this for all sorts of people. You won’t have been the first! 

Having fun, being social and staying in control will keep you on track and add balance and joy to your life. Sounds good to me!

Who’s starting their transformation this January? 🙋‍♀️

#nehawalia #postpartumbody #mumlife❤️ #brisbane #mummytummy

4years ago, the New Year didn’t excite me.
I felt frustrated and lost in my own body.

I was constantly searching online 😞 
how to lose weight fast, jumping from one solution to another.

That’s when I realised something important:
weight loss is one of the most searched health topics every year because so many people feel confused, overwhelmed, and exhausted by mixed advice.

When I finally focused on eating the right portions with real nutrition, consistency, and structure, everything changed.
I lost 14 kgs in 2 months😳 without extremes, starvation, or gimmicks.
More than the weight loss, I finally felt in control of my body and my choices again.

If you’re feeling stuck, especially at the start of a new year,don’t wait for another year to pass.

Comment “RESET” or message me.

I’d love to help you do this the right way 🤍

#nehawalia #weightlossmotivation #NewYearReset #sustainableweightloss #noquickfixes RealResults
